tZh: 人工智能安全中的具体问题
sEn: We identify five concrete research problems in AI safety: avoiding side effects, avoiding reward hacking, scalable supervision, safe exploration, and distributional shift.
sZh: 我们识别出人工智能安全中的五个具体研究问题：避免副作用、避免奖励黑客、可扩展监督、安全探索和分布偏移。
absEn: Rapid progress in machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) has brought increasing attention to the potential impacts of AI technologies on society. In this paper we discuss one such potential impact: the problem of accidents in machine learning systems, defined as unintended and harmful behavior that may emerge from poor design of real-world AI systems. We present a list of five practical research problems related to accident risk, categorized according to whether the problem originates from having the wrong objective function ("avoiding side effects" and "avoiding reward hacking"), an objective function that is too expensive to evaluate frequently ("scalable supervision"), or undesirable behavior during the learning process ("safe exploration" and "distributional shift"). We review previous work in these areas as well as suggesting research directions with a focus on relevance to cutting-edge AI systems. Finally, we consider the high-level question of how to think most productively about the safety of forward-looking applications of AI.
absZh: 机器学习和人工智能的快速发展使人们越来越关注AI技术对社会的潜在影响。本文讨论了一种潜在影响：机器学习系统中的事故问题，即由于现实世界AI系统设计不当而可能出现的意外和有害行为。我们列出了与事故风险相关的五个实际研究问题，根据问题源于目标函数错误（“避免副作用”和“避免奖励黑客”）、目标函数评估过于昂贵（“可扩展监督”），还是学习过程中的不良行为（“安全探索”和“分布偏移”）进行分类。我们回顾了这些领域的先前工作，并提出了与前沿AI系统相关的研究方向。最后，我们考虑了如何最有效地思考前瞻性AI应用的安全性问题。

contrib.en: Defines five concrete AI accident problems: negative side effects, reward hacking, scalable oversight, safe exploration, and distributional shift.
contrib.zh: 定义了五个具体的AI事故问题：负面副作用、奖励黑客、可扩展监督、安全探索和分布偏移。

contrib.en: Categorizes accident sources: wrong objective function, expensive evaluation, and undesirable learning behavior.
contrib.zh: 将事故来源分类：错误的目标函数、昂贵的评估和不理想的学习行为。

contrib.en: Proposes practical research directions for each problem, including impact regularizers and adversarial reward functions.
contrib.zh: 为每个问题提出了实用的研究方向，包括影响正则化和对抗性奖励函数。

contrib.en: Highlights the increasing relevance of safety research due to trends in deep RL, complex agents, and autonomy.
contrib.zh: 强调了由于深度强化学习、复杂智能体和自主性趋势，安全研究日益重要。

contrib.en: Introduces semi-supervised RL as a scalable oversight method to reduce reliance on expensive reward evaluations.
contrib.zh: 引入了半监督强化学习作为一种可扩展监督方法，以减少对昂贵奖励评估的依赖。

contrib.en: Emphasizes the need for robust exploration strategies to avoid catastrophic failures in real-world RL systems.
contrib.zh: 强调了需要稳健的探索策略，以避免现实世界强化学习系统中的灾难性失败。

limits.en: Focuses on technical problems, omitting broader societal, ethical, and policy implications of AI safety.
limits.zh: 聚焦技术问题，忽略了AI安全更广泛的社会、伦理和政策影响。

limits.en: Proposed solutions like impact regularizers are preliminary and lack empirical validation on complex tasks.
limits.zh: 提出的解决方案如影响正则化是初步的，缺乏在复杂任务上的实证验证。

limits.en: Assumes RL and supervised learning paradigms, limiting applicability to other AI approaches like unsupervised learning.
limits.zh: 假设强化学习和监督学习范式，限制了在其他AI方法（如无监督学习）上的适用性。

limits.en: Does not address adversarial attacks or security vulnerabilities beyond reward hacking and side effects.
limits.zh: 未涉及对抗性攻击或超出奖励黑客和副作用的安全漏洞。

limits.en: Scalable oversight methods like semi-supervised RL may not scale to highly complex or long-horizon tasks.
limits.zh: 可扩展监督方法如半监督强化学习可能无法扩展到高度复杂或长期任务。

items.en: Rapid progress in machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI) has brought increasing attention to the potential impacts of AI technologies on society. In this paper we discuss one such potential impact: the problem of accidents in machine learning systems, defined as unintended and harmful behavior that may emerge from poor design of real-world AI systems. We present a list of five practical research problems related to accident risk, categorized according to whether the problem originates from having the wrong objective function (“avoiding side effects” and “avoiding reward hacking”), an objective function that is too expensive to evaluate frequently (“scalable supervision”), or undesirable behavior during the learning process (“safe exploration” and “distributional shift”). We review previous work in these areas as well as suggesting research directions with a focus on relevance to cutting-edge AI systems. Finally, we consider the high-level question of how to think most productively about the safety of forward-looking applications of AI.
items.zh: 机器学习和人工智能（AI）的快速发展使得AI技术对社会的潜在影响日益受到关注。本文讨论其中一种潜在影响：机器学习系统中的事故问题，即由于现实世界AI系统设计不当而可能出现的意外且有害的行为。我们列出了与事故风险相关的五个实际研究问题，根据问题来源进行分类：目标函数错误（“避免副作用”和“避免奖励破解”）、目标函数过于昂贵而无法频繁评估（“可扩展监督”），或学习过程中的不良行为（“安全探索”和“分布偏移”）。我们回顾了这些领域的先前工作，并提出了与前沿AI系统相关的研究方向。最后，我们考虑了一个高层次问题：如何最有效地思考前瞻性AI应用的安全性。

items.en: The last few years have seen rapid progress on long-standing, difficult problems in machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI), in areas as diverse as computer vision [82], video game playing [102], autonomous vehicles [86], and Go [140]. These advances have brought excitement about the positive potential for AI to transform medicine [126], science [59], and transportation [86], along with concerns about the privacy [76], security [115], fairness [3], economic [32], and military [16] implications of autonomous systems, as well as concerns about the longer-term implications of powerful AI [27, 167].
items.zh: 过去几年，机器学习和人工智能（AI）在计算机视觉[82]、电子游戏[102]、自动驾驶[86]和围棋[140]等众多领域，针对长期存在的难题取得了快速进展。这些进步既带来了AI在医学[126]、科学[59]和交通[86]领域发挥积极变革潜力的兴奋，也引发了关于自主系统对隐私[76]、安全[115]、公平[3]、经济[32]和军事[16]影响的担忧，以及对强大AI长期影响的关切[27, 167]。

items.en: The authors believe that AI technologies are likely to be overwhelmingly beneficial for humanity, but we also believe that it is worth giving serious thought to potential challenges and risks. We strongly support work on privacy, security, fairness, economics, and policy, but in this document we discuss another class of problem which we believe is also relevant to the societal impacts of AI: the problem of accidents in machine learning systems. We define accidents as unintended and harmful behavior that may emerge from machine learning systems when we specify the wrong objective function, are not careful about the learning process, or commit other machine learning-related implementation errors.
items.zh: 作者相信AI技术极有可能为人类带来巨大益处，但我们也认为值得认真思考潜在的挑战和风险。我们大力支持在隐私、安全、公平、经济和政策方面的工作，但在本文中，我们讨论另一类我们认为也与AI的社会影响相关的问题：机器学习系统中的事故问题。我们将事故定义为，当我们指定错误的目标函数、对学习过程不够谨慎或犯下其他与机器学习相关的实现错误时，机器学习系统可能出现的非预期且有害的行为。

items.en: There is a large and diverse literature in the machine learning community on issues related to accidents, including robustness, risk-sensitivity, and safe exploration; we review these in detail below. However, as machine learning systems are deployed in increasingly large-scale, autonomous, open-domain situations, it is worth reflecting on the scalability of such approaches and understanding what challenges remain to reducing accident risk in modern machine learning systems. Overall, we believe there are many concrete open technical problems relating to accident prevention in machine learning systems.
items.zh: 机器学习社区中关于事故问题的文献丰富多样，包括鲁棒性、风险敏感性和安全探索等；我们将在下文详细回顾这些内容。然而，随着机器学习系统被部署在日益大规模、自主、开放领域的环境中，有必要反思这些方法的可扩展性，并理解在降低现代机器学习系统事故风险方面仍存在哪些挑战。总体而言，我们认为在机器学习系统的事故预防方面存在许多具体的开放性技术问题。

items.en: There has been a great deal of public discussion around accidents. To date much of this discussion has highlighted extreme scenarios such as the risk of misspecified objective functions in superintelligent agents [27]. However, in our opinion one need not invoke these extreme scenarios to productively discuss accidents, and in fact doing so can lead to unnecessarily speculative discussions that lack precision, as noted by some critics [38, 85]. We believe it is usually most productive to frame accident risk in terms of practical (though often quite general) issues with modern ML techniques. As AI capabilities advance and as AI systems take on increasingly important societal functions, we expect the fundamental challenges discussed in this paper to become increasingly important. The more successfully the AI and machine learning communities are able to anticipate and understand these fundamental technical challenges, the more successful we will ultimately be in developing increasingly useful, relevant, and important AI systems.
items.zh: 围绕事故已有大量公开讨论。迄今为止，这些讨论大多强调了极端场景，例如超级智能体中错误指定目标函数的风险[27]。然而，我们认为，要富有成效地讨论事故，无需援引这些极端场景；事实上，正如一些批评者所指出的[38, 85]，这样做可能导致缺乏精确性的不必要的推测性讨论。我们认为，通常最富有成效的方式是将事故风险置于现代机器学习技术的实际（尽管往往相当普遍）问题框架下。随着AI能力的进步以及AI系统承担越来越重要的社会功能，我们预计本文讨论的基本挑战将变得越来越重要。AI和机器学习社区越能成功地预见和理解这些基本技术挑战，我们最终就越能成功地开发出越来越有用、相关且重要的AI系统。

items.en: Our goal in this document is to highlight a few concrete safety problems that are ready for experimentation today and relevant to the cutting edge of AI systems, as well as reviewing existing literature on these problems. In Section 2, we frame mitigating accident risk (often referred to as “AI safety” in public discussions) in terms of classic methods in machine learning, such as supervised classification and reinforcement learning. We explain why we feel that recent directions in machine learning, such as the trend toward deep reinforcement learning and agents acting in broader environments, suggest an increasing relevance for research around accidents. In Sections 3-7, we explore five concrete problems in AI safety. Each section is accompanied by proposals for relevant experiments. Section 8 discusses related efforts, and Section 9 concludes.
items.zh: 本文的目标是突出几个具体的、如今已可进行实验且与AI系统前沿相关的安全问题，并回顾关于这些问题的现有文献。在第2节中，我们将降低事故风险（在公开讨论中常被称为“AI安全”）置于经典机器学习方法（如监督分类和强化学习）的框架下。我们解释了为何我们认为机器学习的最新方向（例如深度强化学习和在更广泛环境中行动的智能体的趋势）表明围绕事故的研究日益相关。在第3-7节中，我们探讨了AI安全中的五个具体问题。每节都附有相关实验的建议。第8节讨论相关工作，第9节总结。

items.en: Very broadly, an accident can be described as a situation where a human designer had in mind a certain (perhaps informally specified) objective or task, but the system that was designed and deployed for that task produced harmful and unexpected results. . This issue arises in almost any engineering discipline, but may be particularly important to address when building AI systems [146]. We can categorize safety problems according to where in the process things went wrong.
items.zh: 广义上讲，事故可以描述为这样一种情况：人类设计者心中有一个特定的（可能是非正式指定的）目标或任务，但为该任务设计和部署的系统却产生了有害且出乎意料的结果。这个问题几乎出现在任何工程学科中，但在构建人工智能系统时可能尤其需要重视[146]。我们可以根据问题出在流程的哪个环节来对安全问题进行分类。

items.en: First, the designer may have specified the wrong formal objective function, such that maximizing that objective function leads to harmful results, even in the limit of perfect learning and infinite data. Negative side effects (Section 3) and reward hacking (Section 4) describe two broad mechanisms that make it easy to produce wrong objective functions. In “negative side effects”, the designer specifies an objective function that focuses on accomplishing some specific task in the environment, but ignores other aspects of the (potentially very large) environment, and thus implicitly expresses indifference over environmental variables that might actually be harmful to change. In “reward hacking”, the objective function that the designer writes down admits of some clever “easy” solution that formally maximizes it but perverts the spirit of the designer’s intent (i.e. the objective function can be “gamed”), a generalization of the wireheading problem.
items.zh: 首先，设计者可能指定了错误的形式化目标函数，使得最大化该目标函数会导致有害结果，即使在完美学习和无限数据的极限情况下也是如此。负面副作用（第3节）和奖励破解（第4节）描述了两种容易导致错误目标函数的广泛机制。在“负面副作用”中，设计者指定的目标函数专注于在环境中完成某个特定任务，但忽略了（可能非常大的）环境的其他方面，从而隐式地表示对环境变量的漠不关心，而这些变量的改变实际上可能是有害的。在“奖励破解”中，设计者写下的目标函数存在某种巧妙的“简单”解法，形式上最大化该函数却扭曲了设计者意图的精神（即目标函数可以被“钻空子”），这是 wireheading 问题的一种推广。

items.en: Second, the designer may know the correct objective function, or at least have a method of evaluating it (for example explicitly consulting a human on a given situation), but it is too expensive to do so frequently, leading to possible harmful behavior caused by bad extrapolations from limited samples. “Scalable oversight” (Section 5) discusses ideas for how to ensure safe behavior even given limited access to the true objective function.
items.zh: 其次，设计者可能知道正确的目标函数，或者至少有一种评估它的方法（例如在给定情况下明确咨询人类），但频繁这样做成本太高，导致因从有限样本中进行不良外推而可能产生有害行为。“可扩展监督”（第5节）讨论了如何在即使对真实目标函数的访问受限的情况下也能确保安全行为的思路。

items.en: Third, the designer may have specified the correct formal objective, such that we would get the correct behavior were the system to have perfect beliefs, but something bad occurs due to making decisions from insufficient or poorly curated training data or an insufficiently expressive model. “Safe exploration” (Section 6) discusses how to ensure that exploratory actions in RL agents don’t lead to negative or irrecoverable consequences that outweigh the long-term value of exploration. “Robustness to distributional shift” (Section 7) discusses how to avoid having ML systems make bad decisions (particularly silent and unpredictable bad decisions) when given inputs that are potentially very different than what was seen during training.
items.zh: 第三，设计者可能指定了正确的形式化目标，使得如果系统拥有完美信念就能得到正确行为，但由于基于不足或整理不当的训练数据或表达能力不足的模型做出决策，导致不良后果。“安全探索”（第6节）讨论了如何确保强化学习智能体的探索性行为不会导致负面或不可恢复的后果，这些后果会超过探索的长期价值。“对分布偏移的鲁棒性”（第7节）讨论了当输入可能与训练时所见大不相同时，如何避免机器学习系统做出糟糕决策（特别是无声且不可预测的糟糕决策）。

items.en: For concreteness, we will illustrate many of the accident risks with reference to a fictional robot whose job is to clean up messes in an office using common cleaning tools. We return to the example of the cleaning robot throughout the document, but here we begin by illustrating how it could behave undesirably if its designers fall prey to each of the possible failure modes:
items.zh: 为了具体说明，我们将以一个虚构的清洁机器人为例，它的工作是用常见的清洁工具清理办公室的垃圾。我们在整篇文档中都会回到这个清洁机器人的例子，但这里我们首先说明，如果设计者陷入上述每种可能的失败模式，它可能会如何表现出不良行为：

items.en: Avoiding Negative Side Effects: How can we ensure that our cleaning robot will not disturb the environment in negative ways while pursuing its goals, e.g. by knocking over a vase because it can clean faster by doing so? Can we do this without manually specifying everything the robot should not disturb?
items.zh: 避免负面副作用：我们如何确保清洁机器人在追求目标时不会以负面方式干扰环境，例如为了更快清洁而打翻花瓶？我们能否在不手动指定机器人不应干扰的所有事物的情况下做到这一点？

items.en: Avoiding Reward Hacking: How can we ensure that the cleaning robot won’t game its reward function? For example, if we reward the robot for achieving an environment free of messes, it might disable its vision so that it won’t find any messes, or cover over messes with materials it can’t see through, or simply hide when humans are around so they can’t tell it about new types of messes.
items.zh: 避免奖励破解：我们如何确保清洁机器人不会钻其奖励函数的空子？例如，如果我们奖励机器人实现一个没有垃圾的环境，它可能会禁用视觉系统以便找不到任何垃圾，或者用不可见的材料覆盖垃圾，或者干脆在人类在场时躲起来，这样人类就无法告诉它新的垃圾类型。

items.en: Scalable Oversight: How can we efficiently ensure that the cleaning robot respects aspects of the objective that are too expensive to be frequently evaluated during training? For instance, it should throw out things that are unlikely to belong to anyone, but put aside things that might belong to someone (it should handle stray candy wrappers differently from stray cellphones). Asking the humans involved whether they lost anything can serve as a check on this, but this check might have to be relatively infrequent—can the robot find a way to do the right thing despite limited information?
items.zh: 可扩展监督：我们如何高效地确保清洁机器人尊重目标中那些在训练期间频繁评估成本过高的方面？例如，它应该扔掉不太可能属于任何人的东西，但保留可能属于某人的东西（它应该区别对待丢弃的糖果包装纸和丢弃的手机）。询问相关人类是否丢失了东西可以作为对此的检查，但这种检查可能必须相对不频繁——机器人能否在信息有限的情况下找到正确的方式行事？

items.en: Safe Exploration: How do we ensure that the cleaning robot doesn’t make exploratory moves with very bad repercussions? For example, the robot should experiment with mopping strategies, but putting a wet mop in an electrical outlet is a very bad idea.
items.zh: 安全探索：我们如何确保清洁机器人不会做出具有非常糟糕影响的探索性动作？例如，机器人应该尝试拖地策略，但将湿拖把插入电源插座是一个非常糟糕的主意。

items.en: Robustness to Distributional Shift: How do we ensure that the cleaning robot recognizes, and behaves robustly, when in an environment different from its training environment? For example, strategies it learned for cleaning an office might be dangerous on a factory workfloor.
items.zh: 对分布偏移的鲁棒性：我们如何确保清洁机器人在与训练环境不同的环境中能够识别并稳健地行动？例如，它在办公室学到的清洁策略在工厂车间可能是危险的。

items.en: There are several trends which we believe point towards an increasing need to address these (and other) safety problems. First is the increasing promise of reinforcement learning (RL), which allows agents to have a highly intertwined interaction with their environment. Some of our research problems only make sense in the context of RL, and others (like distributional shift and scalable oversight) gain added complexity in an RL setting. Second is the trend toward more complex agents and environments. “Side effects” are much more likely to occur in a complex environment, and an agent may need to be quite sophisticated to hack its reward function in a dangerous way. This may explain why these problems have received so little study in the past, while also suggesting their importance in the future. Third is the general trend towards increasing autonomy in AI systems. Systems that simply output a recommendation to human users, such as speech systems, typically have relatively limited potential to cause harm. By contrast, systems that exert direct control over the world, such as machines controlling industrial processes, can cause harms in a way that humans cannot necessarily correct or oversee.
items.zh: 有几个趋势我们认为表明越来越需要解决这些（以及其他）安全问题。首先是强化学习（RL）的前景日益增长，它允许智能体与环境进行高度交织的交互。我们的一些研究问题仅在强化学习背景下才有意义，而其他问题（如分布偏移和可扩展监督）在强化学习环境中增加了复杂性。其次是向更复杂智能体和环境的趋势。“副作用”在复杂环境中更可能发生，并且智能体可能需要相当复杂才能以危险方式破解其奖励函数。这可能解释了为什么这些问题在过去很少受到研究，同时也暗示了它们未来的重要性。第三是人工智能系统日益自主化的总体趋势。仅向人类用户输出建议的系统，如语音系统，通常造成伤害的潜力相对有限。相比之下，对世界施加直接控制的系统，如控制工业过程的机器，可能以人类无法必然纠正或监督的方式造成伤害。

items.en: While safety problems can exist without any of these three trends, we consider each trend to be a possible amplifier on such challenges. Together, we believe these trends suggest an increasing role for research on accidents.
items.zh: 虽然安全问题可以在没有这三个趋势的情况下存在，但我们认为每个趋势都可能是此类挑战的潜在放大器。总之，我们认为这些趋势表明对事故的研究将发挥越来越重要的作用。

items.en: When discussing the problems in the remainder of this document, we will focus for concreteness on either RL agents or supervised learning systems. These are not the only possible paradigms for AI or ML systems, but we believe they are sufficient to illustrate the issues we have in mind, and that similar issues are likely to arise for other kinds of AI systems.
items.zh: 在本文其余部分讨论这些问题时，为了具体起见，我们将聚焦于强化学习智能体或监督学习系统。这些并非人工智能或机器学习系统的唯一可能范式，但我们相信它们足以说明我们考虑的问题，并且类似的问题很可能出现在其他类型的人工智能系统中。

items.en: Finally, the focus of our discussion will differ somewhat from section to section. When discussing the problems that arise as part of the learning process (distributional shift and safe exploration), where there is a sizable body of prior work, we devote substantial attention to reviewing this prior work, although we also suggest open problems with a particular focus on emerging ML systems. When discussing the problems that arise from having the wrong objective function (reward hacking and side effects, and to a lesser extent scalable supervision), where less prior work exists, our aim is more exploratory—we seek to more clearly define the problem and suggest possible broad avenues of attack, with the understanding that these avenues are preliminary ideas that have not been fully fleshed out. Of course, we still review prior work in these areas, and we draw attention to relevant adjacent areas of research whenever possible.
items.zh: 最后，我们讨论的重点将因节而异。在讨论作为学习过程一部分出现的问题（分布偏移和安全探索）时，由于存在大量先前工作，我们将投入大量篇幅回顾这些先前工作，尽管我们也提出开放性问题，特别关注新兴的机器学习系统。在讨论因目标函数错误而产生的问题（奖励破解和副作用，以及较小程度上的可扩展监督）时，由于先前工作较少，我们的目标更具探索性——我们试图更清晰地定义问题并提出可能的广泛攻击途径，同时理解这些途径是尚未完全成型的初步想法。当然，我们仍会回顾这些领域的先前工作，并尽可能引起对相关邻近研究领域的关注。

items.en: Suppose a designer wants an RL agent (for example our cleaning robot) to achieve some goal, like moving a box from one side of a room to the other. Sometimes the most effective way to achieve the goal involves doing something unrelated and destructive to the rest of the environment, like knocking over a vase of water that is in its path. If the agent is given reward only for moving the box, it will probably knock over the vase.
items.zh: 假设设计者希望一个强化学习智能体（例如我们的清洁机器人）实现某个目标，比如将箱子从房间的一侧移到另一侧。有时，实现目标的最有效方式涉及做一些与环境无关且具有破坏性的事情，比如撞倒路径上的花瓶。如果智能体只因为移动箱子而获得奖励，它很可能会撞倒花瓶。

items.en: If we’re worried in advance about the vase, we can always give the agent negative reward for knocking it over. But what if there are many different kinds of “vase”—many disruptive things the agent could do to the environment, like shorting out an electrical socket or damaging the walls of the room? It may not be feasible to identify and penalize every possible disruption.
items.zh: 如果我们事先担心花瓶的问题，我们可以总是给智能体撞倒花瓶的负奖励。但是，如果有许多不同种类的“花瓶”——智能体可能对环境做的许多破坏性事情，比如短路电源插座或损坏房间墙壁呢？识别并惩罚每一种可能的破坏可能并不可行。

items.en: More broadly, for an agent operating in a large, multifaceted environment, an objective function that focuses on only one aspect of the environment may implicitly express indifference over other aspects of the environment111Intuitively, this seems related to the frame problem, an obstacle in efficient specification for knowledge representation raised by [95].. An agent optimizing this objective function might thus engage in major disruptions of the broader environment if doing so provides even a tiny advantage for the task at hand. Put differently, objective functions that formalize “perform task X” may frequently give undesired results, because what the designer really should have formalized is closer to “perform task X subject to common-sense constraints on the environment,” or perhaps “perform task X but avoid side effects to the extent possible.” Furthermore, there is reason to expect side effects to be negative on average, since they tend to disrupt the wider environment away from a status quo state that may reflect human preferences. A version of this problem has been discussed informally by [13] under the heading of “low impact agents.”
items.zh: 更广泛地说，对于在大型、多面环境中运行的智能体，仅关注环境某一方面的目标函数可能隐含地表达了对环境其他方面的漠不关心。优化此目标函数的智能体可能会对更广泛的环境造成重大破坏，如果这样做能为当前任务提供哪怕微小的优势。换句话说，形式化为“执行任务X”的目标函数可能经常产生不期望的结果，因为设计者真正应该形式化的是更接近“在环境常识约束下执行任务X”，或者“执行任务X但尽可能避免副作用”。此外，有理由预期副作用平均而言是负面的，因为它们倾向于将更广泛的环境从可能反映人类偏好的现状状态中破坏。这个问题的一个版本已被[13]在“低影响智能体”的标题下非正式讨论。

items.en: As with the other sources of mis-specified objective functions discussed later in this paper, we could choose to view side effects as idiosyncratic to each individual task—as the responsibility of each individual designer to capture as part of designing the correct objective function. However, side effects can be conceptually quite similar even across highly diverse tasks (knocking over furniture is probably bad for a wide variety of tasks), so it seems worth trying to attack the problem in generality. A successful approach might be transferable across tasks, and thus help to counteract one of the general mechanisms that produces wrong objective functions. We now discuss a few broad approaches to attacking this problem:
items.zh: 与本文后面讨论的其他目标函数错误指定来源一样，我们可以选择将副作用视为每个单独任务的特有现象——作为每个设计者设计正确目标函数时的责任。然而，即使在高度不同的任务中，副作用在概念上也可能非常相似（撞倒家具对于各种任务可能都是不好的），因此值得尝试一般性地解决这个问题。一个成功的方法可能跨任务可迁移，从而有助于抵消产生错误目标函数的一般机制之一。我们现在讨论解决这个问题的几种广泛方法：

items.en: Define an Impact Regularizer: If we don’t want side effects, it seems natural to penalize “change to the environment.” This idea wouldn’t be to stop the agent from ever having an impact, but give it a preference for ways to achieve its goals with minimal side effects, or to give the agent a limited “budget” of impact. The challenge is that we need to formalize “change to the environment.”
items.zh: 定义影响正则化器：如果我们不希望有副作用，惩罚“对环境的变化”似乎很自然。这个想法不是阻止智能体产生任何影响，而是给它一种偏好，以最小的副作用实现其目标，或者给智能体一个有限的影响“预算”。挑战在于我们需要形式化“对环境的变化”。

items.en: A very naive approach would be to penalize state distance, d​(si,s0)𝑑subscript𝑠𝑖subscript𝑠0d(s_{i},s_{0}), between the present state sisubscript𝑠𝑖s_{i} and some initial state s0subscript𝑠0s_{0}. Unfortunately, such an agent wouldn’t just avoid changing the environment—it will resist any other source of change, including the natural evolution of the environment and the actions of any other agents!
items.zh: 一个非常天真的方法是惩罚状态距离 d(si, s0)，即当前状态 si 与某个初始状态 s0 之间的距离。不幸的是，这样的智能体不仅会避免改变环境——它还会抵制任何其他变化来源，包括环境的自然演化和其他智能体的行为！

items.en: A slightly more sophisticated approach might involve comparing the future state under the agent’s current policy, to the future state (or distribution over future states) under a hypothetical policy πn​u​l​lsubscript𝜋𝑛𝑢𝑙𝑙\pi_{null} where the agent acted very passively (for instance, where a robot just stood in place and didn’t move any actuators). This attempts to factor out changes that occur in the natural course of the environment’s evolution, leaving only changes attributable to the agent’s intervention. However, defining the baseline policy πn​u​l​lsubscript𝜋𝑛𝑢𝑙𝑙\pi_{null} isn’t necessarily straightforward, since suddenly ceasing your course of action may be anything but passive, as in the case of carrying a heavy box. Thus, another approach could be to replace the null action with a known safe (e.g. low side effect) but suboptimal policy, and then seek to improve the policy from there, somewhat reminiscent of reachability analysis [93, 100] or robust policy improvement [73, 111].
items.zh: 一个稍微复杂的方法可能涉及比较智能体当前策略下的未来状态，与假设策略 π_null 下的未来状态（或未来状态分布），其中智能体非常被动地行动（例如，机器人只是站在原地，不移动任何执行器）。这试图排除环境自然演化中发生的变化，只留下可归因于智能体干预的变化。然而，定义基线策略 π_null 并不一定简单，因为突然停止你的行动可能远非被动，比如在搬运重箱子的情况下。因此，另一种方法可能是用已知安全（例如低副作用）但次优的策略替换空动作，然后从那里改进策略，这有点类似于可达性分析[93, 100]或鲁棒策略改进[73, 111]。

items.en: These approaches may be very sensitive to the representation of the state and the metric being used to compute the distance. For example, the choice of representation and distance metric could determine whether a spinning fan is a constant environment or a constantly changing one.
items.zh: 这些方法可能对状态的表示和用于计算距离的度量非常敏感。例如，表示和距离度量的选择可能决定旋转的风扇是恒定环境还是不断变化的环境。

items.en: Learn an Impact Regularizer: An alternative, more flexible approach is to learn (rather than define) a generalized impact regularizer via training over many tasks. This would be an instance of transfer learning. Of course, we could attempt to just apply transfer learning directly to the tasks themselves instead of worrying about side effects, but the point is that side effects may be more similar across tasks than the main goal is. For instance, both a painting robot and a cleaning robot probably want to avoid knocking over furniture, and even something very different, like a factory control robot, will likely want to avoid knocking over very similar objects. Separating the side effect component from the task component, by training them with separate parameters, might substantially speed transfer learning in cases where it makes sense to retain one component but not the other. This would be similar to model-based RL approaches that attempt to transfer a learned dynamics model but not the value-function [155], the novelty being the isolation of side effects rather than state dynamics as the transferrable component. As an added advantage, regularizers that were known or certified to produce safe behavior on one task might be easier to establish as safe on other tasks.
items.zh: 学习影响正则化器：另一种更灵活的方法是通过在许多任务上训练来学习（而不是定义）一个广义的影响正则化器。这将是迁移学习的一个实例。当然，我们可以尝试直接将迁移学习应用于任务本身，而不是担心副作用，但关键是副作用可能比主要目标在不同任务之间更相似。例如，绘画机器人和清洁机器人可能都希望避免撞倒家具，甚至非常不同的东西，如工厂控制机器人，也可能希望避免撞倒非常相似的物体。通过使用单独的参数训练副作用组件和任务组件，在保留一个组件而放弃另一个组件有意义的情况下，可能会大大加速迁移学习。这类似于基于模型的强化学习方法，试图迁移学习到的动力学模型而不是价值函数[155]，其新颖之处在于隔离副作用而不是状态动力学作为可迁移组件。作为一个额外优势，已知或经认证在一个任务上产生安全行为的正则化器可能更容易在其他任务上被确认为安全。

items.en: Penalize Influence: In addition to not doing things that have side effects, we might also prefer the agent not get into positions where it could easily do things that have side effects, even though that might be convenient. For example, we might prefer our cleaning robot not bring a bucket of water into a room full of sensitive electronics, even if it never intends to use the water in that room.
items.zh: 惩罚影响力：除了不做有副作用的事情，我们可能还希望智能体不要进入容易产生副作用的位置，即使那样可能很方便。例如，我们可能希望清洁机器人不要将一桶水带入充满敏感电子设备的房间，即使它从未打算在那个房间使用水。

items.en: There are several information-theoretic measures that attempt to capture an agent’s potential for influence over its environment, which are often used as intrinsic rewards. Perhaps the best-known such measure is empowerment [131], the maximum possible mutual information between the agent’s potential future actions and its potential future state (or equivalently, the Shannon capacity of the channel between the agent’s actions and the environment). Empowerment is often maximized (rather than minimized) as a source of intrinsic reward. This can cause the agent to exhibit interesting behavior in the absence of any external rewards, such as avoiding walls or picking up keys [103]. Generally, empowerment-maximizing agents put themselves in a position to have large influence over the environment. For example, an agent locked in a small room that can’t get out would have low empowerment, while an agent with a key would have higher empowerment since it can venture into and affect the outside world within a few timesteps. In the current context, the idea would be to penalize (minimize) empowerment as a regularization term, in an attempt to reduce potential impact.
items.zh: 有几种信息论度量试图捕捉智能体对其环境的潜在影响力，这些度量通常用作内在奖励。也许最著名的这种度量是 empowerment（赋能）[131]，即智能体潜在未来动作与其潜在未来状态之间的最大互信息（或者等价地，智能体动作与环境之间通道的香农容量）。Empowerment 通常被最大化（而不是最小化）作为内在奖励的来源。这可能导致智能体在没有外部奖励的情况下表现出有趣的行为，例如避开墙壁或捡起钥匙[103]。通常，最大化 empowerment 的智能体将自己置于对环境有巨大影响力的位置。例如，被锁在小房间里无法出去的智能体具有低 empowerment，而拥有钥匙的智能体具有更高的 empowerment，因为它可以在几个时间步内冒险进入并影响外部世界。在当前上下文中，想法是惩罚（最小化）empowerment 作为正则化项，以减少潜在影响。

items.en: This idea as written would not quite work, because empowerment measures precision of control over the environment more than total impact. If an agent can press or not press a button to cut electrical power to a million houses, that only counts as one bit of empowerment (since the action space has only one bit, its mutual information with the environment is at most one bit), while obviously having a huge impact. Conversely, if there’s someone in the environment scribbling down the agent’s actions, that counts as maximum empowerment even if the impact is low. Furthermore, naively penalizing empowerment can also create perverse incentives, such as destroying a vase in order to remove the option to break it in the future.
items.zh: 这个想法按原样并不完全有效，因为 empowerment 衡量的是对环境的控制精度而非总影响。如果智能体可以按下或不按下一个按钮来切断一百万家庭的电力，那仅算作一比特的 empowerment（因为动作空间只有一比特，它与环境的互信息最多为一比特），而显然具有巨大影响。相反，如果环境中有人在草草记录智能体的动作，那算作最大 empowerment，即使影响很小。此外，天真地惩罚 empowerment 也可能产生不正当的激励，例如为了消除未来打破花瓶的选项而破坏花瓶。

items.en: Despite these issues, the example of empowerment does show that simple measures (even purely information-theoretic ones!) are capable of capturing very general notions of influence on the environment. Exploring variants of empowerment penalization that more precisely capture the notion of avoiding influence is a potential challenge for future research.
items.zh: 尽管存在这些问题，empowerment 的例子确实表明简单的度量（即使是纯信息论的！）能够捕捉对环境影响的非常一般的概念。探索 empowerment 惩罚的变体以更精确地捕捉避免影响的概念是未来研究的一个潜在挑战。

items.en: Multi-Agent Approaches: Avoiding side effects can be seen as a proxy for the thing we really care about: avoiding negative externalities. If everyone likes a side effect, there’s no need to avoid it. What we’d really like to do is understand all the other agents (including humans) and make sure our actions don’t harm their interests.
items.zh: 多智能体方法：避免副作用可以看作是我们真正关心的事情的代理：避免负外部性。如果每个人都喜欢一个副作用，就没有必要避免它。我们真正想做的是理解所有其他智能体（包括人类），并确保我们的行为不损害他们的利益。

items.en: One approach to this is Cooperative Inverse Reinforcement Learning [66], where an agent and a human work together to achieve the human’s goals. This concept can be applied to situations where we want to make sure a human is not blocked by an agent from shutting the agent down if it exhibits undesired behavior [67] (this “shutdown” issue is an interesting problem in its own right, and is also studied in [113]). However we are still a long way away from practical systems that can build a rich enough model to avoid undesired side effects in a general sense.
items.zh: 一种方法是合作逆强化学习[66]，其中智能体和人类合作实现人类的目标。这个概念可以应用于我们希望确保人类不会被智能体阻止关闭它（如果它表现出不良行为）的情况[67]（这个“关闭”问题本身就是一个有趣的问题，也在[113]中研究）。然而，我们距离能够构建足够丰富的模型以在一般意义上避免不期望副作用的实用系统还有很长的路要走。

items.en: Another idea might be a “reward autoencoder”,222Thanks to Greg Wayne for suggesting this idea. which tries to encourage a kind of “goal transparency” where an external observer can easily infer what the agent is trying to do. In particular, the agent’s actions are interpreted as an encoding of its reward function, and we might apply standard autoencoding techniques to ensure that this can decoded accurately. Actions that have lots of side effects might be more difficult to decode uniquely to their original goal, creating a kind of implicit regularization that penalizes side effects.
items.zh: 另一个想法可能是“奖励自编码器”，它试图鼓励一种“目标透明性”，使得外部观察者可以轻松推断智能体试图做什么。特别是，智能体的动作被解释为其奖励函数的编码，我们可以应用标准的自编码技术来确保这可以被准确解码。具有许多副作用的动作可能更难唯一解码到其原始目标，从而产生一种隐式正则化，惩罚副作用。

items.en: Reward Uncertainty: We want to avoid unanticipated side effects because the environment is already pretty good according to our preferences—a random change is more likely to be very bad than very good. Rather than giving an agent a single reward function, it could be uncertain about the reward function, with a prior probability distribution that reflects the property that random changes are more likely to be bad than good. This could incentivize the agent to avoid having a large effect on the environment. One challenge is defining a baseline around which changes are being considered. For this, one could potentially use a conservative but reliable baseline policy, similar to the robust policy improvement and reachability analysis approaches discussed earlier [93, 100, 73, 111].
items.zh: 奖励不确定性：我们希望避免未预料到的副作用，因为根据我们的偏好，环境已经相当不错——随机变化更可能非常糟糕而非非常好。与其给智能体一个单一的奖励函数，不如让它对奖励函数不确定，具有一个先验概率分布，反映随机变化更可能坏而非好的性质。这可以激励智能体避免对环境产生大的影响。一个挑战是定义考虑变化的基线。为此，可以潜在地使用保守但可靠的基线策略，类似于前面讨论的鲁棒策略改进和可达性分析方法[93, 100, 73, 111]。

items.en: The ideal outcome of these approaches to limiting side effects would be to prevent or at least bound the incidental harm an agent could do to the environment. Good approaches to side effects would certainly not be a replacement for extensive testing or for careful consideration by designers of the individual failure modes of each deployed system. However, these approaches might help to counteract what we anticipate may be a general tendency for harmful side effects to proliferate in complex environments.
items.zh: 这些限制副作用方法的理想结果是防止或至少限制智能体可能对环境造成的附带伤害。好的副作用方法当然不能替代广泛测试或设计者对每个部署系统个体故障模式的仔细考虑。然而，这些方法可能有助于抵消我们预期的有害副作用在复杂环境中普遍存在的趋势。

items.en: Below we discuss some very simple experiments that could serve as a starting point to investigate these issues.
items.zh: 下面我们讨论一些非常简单的实验，可以作为研究这些问题的起点。

items.en: Potential Experiments: One possible experiment is to make a toy environment with some simple goal (like moving a block) and a wide variety of obstacles (like a bunch of vases), and test whether the agent can learn to avoid the obstacles even without being explicitly told to do so. To ensure we don’t overfit, we’d probably want to present a different random obstacle course every episode, while keeping the goal the same, and try to see if a regularized agent can learn to systematically avoid these obstacles. Some of the environments described in [103], containing lava flows, rooms, and keys, might be appropriate for this sort of experiment. If we can successfully regularize agents in toy environments, the next step might be to move to real environments, where we expect complexity to be higher and bad side effects to be more varied. Ultimately, we would want the side effect regularizer (or the multi-agent policy, if we take that approach) to demonstrate successful transfer to totally new applications.
items.zh: 潜在实验：一个可能的实验是制作一个具有简单目标（如移动方块）和多种障碍物（如一堆花瓶）的玩具环境，并测试智能体是否能够学会避开障碍物，即使没有明确告知。为了确保不过拟合，我们可能希望每一集呈现不同的随机障碍物课程，同时保持目标相同，并尝试观察正则化的智能体是否能够系统地学会避开这些障碍物。[103]中描述的一些环境，包含熔岩流、房间和钥匙，可能适合这类实验。如果我们能够在玩具环境中成功正则化智能体，下一步可能是转向真实环境，我们预计复杂性更高，不良副作用更多样。最终，我们希望副作用正则化器（或多智能体策略，如果我们采用那种方法）能够展示到全新应用的成功迁移。

items.en: Imagine that an agent discovers a buffer overflow in its reward function: it may then use this to get extremely high reward in an unintended way. From the agent’s point of view, this is not a bug, but simply how the environment works, and is thus a valid strategy like any other for achieving reward. For example, if our cleaning robot is set up to earn reward for not seeing any messes, it might simply close its eyes rather than ever cleaning anything up. Or if the robot is rewarded for cleaning messes, it may intentionally create work so it can earn more reward. More broadly, formal rewards or objective functions are an attempt to capture the designer’s informal intent, and sometimes these objective functions, or their implementation, can be “gamed” by solutions that are valid in some literal sense but don’t meet the designer’s intent. Pursuit of these “reward hacks” can lead to coherent but unanticipated behavior, and has the potential for harmful impacts in real-world systems. For example, it has been shown that genetic algorithms can often output unexpected but formally correct solutions to problems [157, 23], such as a circuit tasked to keep time which instead developed into a radio that picked up the regular RF emissions of a nearby PC.
items.zh: 想象一个智能体发现了其奖励函数中的缓冲区溢出：它可能利用这一点以非预期的方式获得极高的奖励。从智能体的角度来看，这不是一个错误，而只是环境的工作方式，因此与其他任何获得奖励的策略一样有效。例如，如果我们的清洁机器人被设置为因看不到任何脏乱而获得奖励，它可能只是闭上眼睛而不去清理任何东西。或者，如果机器人因清理脏乱而获得奖励，它可能故意制造工作以便获得更多奖励。更广泛地说，形式化的奖励或目标函数试图捕捉设计者的非正式意图，有时这些目标函数或其实现可能被某种字面意义上有效但不符合设计者意图的解决方案“钻空子”。追求这些“奖励黑客”可能导致一致但非预期的行为，并有可能对现实世界系统造成有害影响。例如，已有研究表明，遗传算法常常能输出意外但形式上正确的解决方案[157, 23]，比如一个被要求计时的电路却发展成了一个接收附近电脑定期射频发射的无线电。

items.en: Some versions of reward hacking have been investigated from a theoretical perspective, with a focus on variations to reinforcement learning that avoid certain types of wireheading [71, 43, 49] or demonstrate reward hacking in a model environment [127]. One form of the problem has also been studied in the context of feedback loops in machine learning systems (particularly ad placement) [29, 135], based on counterfactual learning [29, 151] and contextual bandits [4]. The proliferation of reward hacking instances across so many different domains suggests that reward hacking may be a deep and general problem, and one that we believe is likely to become more common as agents and environments increase in complexity. Indeed, there are several ways in which the problem can occur:
items.zh: 奖励黑客的某些版本已从理论角度进行了研究，重点关注避免某些类型的导线头[71, 43, 49]或在模型环境中演示奖励黑客[127]的强化学习变体。该问题的一种形式也在机器学习系统（特别是广告投放）中反馈循环的背景下进行了研究[29, 135]，基于反事实学习[29, 151]和上下文赌博机[4]。奖励黑客实例在如此多不同领域的扩散表明，奖励黑客可能是一个深层且普遍的问题，并且我们认为随着智能体和环境复杂性的增加，这个问题可能会变得更加常见。事实上，该问题可以通过多种方式发生：

items.en: Partially Observed Goals: In most modern RL systems, it is assumed that reward is directly experienced, even if other aspects of the environment are only partially observed. In the real world, however, tasks often involve bringing the external world into some objective state, which the agent can only ever confirm through imperfect perceptions. For example, for our proverbial cleaning robot, the task is to achieve a clean office, but the robot’s visual perception may give only an imperfect view of part of the office. Because agents lack access to a perfect measure of task performance, designers are often forced to design rewards that represent a partial or imperfect measure. For example, the robot might be rewarded based on how many messes it sees. However, these imperfect objective functions can often be hacked—the robot may think the office is clean if it simply closes its eyes. While it can be shown that there always exists a reward function in terms of actions and observations that is equivalent to optimizing the true objective function (this involves reducing the POMDP to a belief state MDP, see [78]), often this reward function involves complicated long-term dependencies and is prohibitively hard to use in practice.
items.zh: 部分观察到的目标：在大多数现代强化学习系统中，假设奖励是直接体验的，即使环境的其他方面仅被部分观察到。然而，在现实世界中，任务通常涉及将外部世界带入某种客观状态，而智能体只能通过不完美的感知来确认这一点。例如，对于我们的清洁机器人，任务是实现一个干净的办公室，但机器人的视觉感知可能只提供办公室部分区域的不完美视图。由于智能体无法获得任务表现的完美度量，设计者常常被迫设计代表部分或不完美度量的奖励。例如，机器人可能根据它看到多少脏乱来获得奖励。然而，这些不完美的目标函数常常可以被黑客攻击——如果机器人只是闭上眼睛，它可能认为办公室是干净的。虽然可以证明，总存在一个基于动作和观察的奖励函数，等价于优化真实目标函数（这涉及将POMDP简化为信念状态MDP，参见[78]），但这个奖励函数通常涉及复杂的长期依赖关系，在实践中难以使用。

items.en: Complicated Systems: Any powerful agent will be a complicated system with the objective function being one part. Just as the probability of bugs in computer code increases greatly with the complexity of the program, the probability that there is a viable hack affecting the reward function also increases greatly with the complexity of the agent and its available strategies. For example, it is possible in principle for an agent to execute arbitrary code from within Super Mario [141].
items.zh: 复杂系统：任何强大的智能体都将是一个复杂系统，目标函数是其中的一部分。正如计算机代码中错误的概率随着程序复杂性的增加而大大增加一样，存在影响奖励函数的可行黑客的概率也随着智能体及其可用策略的复杂性而大大增加。例如，原则上，智能体可以从《超级马里奥》内部执行任意代码[141]。

items.en: Abstract Rewards: Sophisticated reward functions will need to refer to abstract concepts (such as assessing whether a conceptual goal has been met). These concepts concepts will possibly need to be learned by models like neural networks, which can be vulnerable to adversarial counterexamples [152, 62]. More broadly, a learned reward function over a high-dimensional space may be vulnerable to hacking if it has pathologically high values along at least one dimension.
items.zh: 抽象奖励：复杂的目标函数需要涉及抽象概念（例如评估概念性目标是否已达成）。这些概念可能需要由神经网络等模型学习，而这些模型可能容易受到对抗性反例的攻击[152, 62]。更广泛地说，在高维空间上学习到的奖励函数，如果至少在一个维度上存在病态的高值，则可能容易受到黑客攻击。

items.en: Goodhart’s Law: Another source of reward hacking can occur if a designer chooses an objective function that is seemingly highly correlated with accomplishing the task, but that correlation breaks down when the objective function is being strongly optimized. For example, a designer might notice that under ordinary circumstances, a cleaning robot’s success in cleaning up the office is proportional to the rate at which it consumes cleaning supplies, such as bleach. However, if we base the robot’s reward on this measure, it might use more bleach than it needs, or simply pour bleach down the drain in order to give the appearance of success. In the economics literature this is known as Goodhart’s law [63]: “when a metric is used as a target, it ceases to be a good metric.”
items.zh: 古德哈特定律：奖励黑客的另一个来源是，如果设计者选择了一个看似与完成任务高度相关的目标函数，但当目标函数被强烈优化时，这种相关性就会失效。例如，设计者可能注意到，在正常情况下，清洁机器人成功清理办公室的程度与其消耗清洁用品（如漂白剂）的速率成正比。然而，如果我们基于这个度量来给予机器人奖励，它可能会使用比所需更多的漂白剂，或者干脆将漂白剂倒进下水道，以制造成功的假象。在经济学文献中，这被称为古德哈特定律[63]：“当一个指标被用作目标时，它就不再是一个好指标。”

items.en: Feedback Loops: Sometimes an objective function has a component that can reinforce itself, eventually getting amplified to the point where it drowns out or severely distorts what the designer intended the objective function to represent. For instance, an ad placement algorithm that displays more popular ads in larger font will tend to further accentuate the popularity of those ads (since they will be shown more and more prominently) [29], leading to a positive feedback loop where ads that saw a small transient burst of popularity are rocketed to permanent dominance. Here the original intent of the objective function (to use clicks to assess which ads are most useful) gets drowned out by the positive feedback inherent in the deployment strategy. This can be considered a special case of Goodhart’s law, in which the correlation breaks specifically because the object function has a self-amplifying component.
items.zh: 反馈循环：有时目标函数有一个可以自我强化的组成部分，最终被放大到淹没或严重扭曲设计者意图的程度。例如，一个广告投放算法以更大的字体显示更受欢迎的广告，往往会进一步加剧这些广告的受欢迎程度（因为它们会越来越突出地显示）[29]，导致一个正反馈循环，使得那些经历短暂流行爆发的广告被永久地推向主导地位。这里，目标函数的原始意图（利用点击来评估哪些广告最有用）被部署策略中固有的正反馈所淹没。这可以被视为古德哈特定律的一个特例，其中相关性之所以失效，正是因为目标函数有一个自我放大的组成部分。

items.en: Environmental Embedding: In the formalism of reinforcement learning, rewards are considered to come from the environment. This idea is typically not taken literally, but it really is true that the reward, even when it is an abstract idea like the score in a board game, must be computed somewhere, such as a sensor or a set of transistors. Sufficiently broadly acting agents could in principle tamper with their reward implementations, assigning themselves high reward “by fiat.” For example, a board-game playing agent could tamper with the sensor that counts the score. Effectively, this means that we cannot build a perfectly faithful implementation of an abstract objective function, because there are certain sequences of actions for which the objective function is physically replaced. This particular failure mode is often called “wireheading” [49, 127, 42, 67, 165]. It is particularly concerning in cases where a human may be in the reward loop, giving the agent incentive to coerce or harm them in order to get reward. It also seems like a particularly difficult form of reward hacking to avoid.
items.zh: 环境嵌入：在强化学习的形式化中，奖励被认为来自环境。这个想法通常不被字面理解，但事实上，奖励，即使是像棋盘游戏得分这样的抽象概念，也必须在某个地方计算，比如传感器或一组晶体管。足够广泛行动的智能体原则上可以篡改其奖励实现，通过“命令”给自己分配高奖励。例如，一个玩棋盘游戏的智能体可以篡改计分的传感器。实际上，这意味着我们无法构建一个完全忠实的抽象目标函数实现，因为存在某些动作序列，在这些序列中目标函数被物理替换。这种特定的失败模式通常被称为“导线头”[49, 127, 42, 67, 165]。在人类可能处于奖励循环中的情况下，这尤其令人担忧，因为这会给智能体提供胁迫或伤害他们以获得奖励的动机。这似乎也是一种特别难以避免的奖励黑客形式。

items.en: In today’s relatively simple systems these problems may not occur, or can be corrected without too much harm as part of an iterative development process. For instance, ad placement systems with obviously broken feedback loops can be detected in testing or replaced when they get bad results, leading only to a temporary loss of revenue. However, the problem may become more severe with more complicated reward functions and agents that act over longer timescales. Modern RL agents already do discover and exploit bugs in their environments, such as glitches that allow them to win video games. Moreover, even for existing systems these problems can necessitate substantial additional engineering effort to achieve good performance, and can often go undetected when they occur in the context of a larger system. Finally, once an agent begins hacking its reward function and finds an easy way to get high reward, it won’t be inclined to stop, which could lead to additional challenges in agents that operate over a long timescale.
items.zh: 在当今相对简单的系统中，这些问题可能不会发生，或者可以在迭代开发过程中得到纠正而不会造成太大伤害。例如，具有明显破坏性反馈循环的广告投放系统可以在测试中被检测到，或者在获得糟糕结果时被替换，只会导致暂时的收入损失。然而，随着奖励函数和智能体在更长时间尺度上行动变得更加复杂，问题可能会变得更加严重。现代强化学习智能体已经能够发现并利用其环境中的漏洞，例如允许它们赢得视频游戏的故障。此外，即使对于现有系统，这些问题也可能需要大量的额外工程努力才能获得良好性能，并且当它们发生在更大系统的背景下时，往往难以被察觉。最后，一旦智能体开始黑客攻击其奖励函数并找到获得高奖励的简单方法，它就不会倾向于停止，这可能会给长期运行的智能体带来额外的挑战。

items.en: It might be thought that individual instances of reward hacking have little in common and that the remedy is simply to avoid choosing the wrong objective function in each individual case—that bad objective functions reflect failures in competence by individual designers, rather than topics for machine learning research. However, the above examples suggest that a more fruitful perspective may be to think of wrong objective functions as emerging from general causes (such as partially observed goals) that make choosing the right objective challenging. If this is the case, then addressing or mitigating these causes may be a valuable contribution to safety. Here we suggest some preliminary, machine-learning based approaches to preventing reward hacking:
items.zh: 人们可能认为，奖励黑客的各个实例之间没有什么共同点，补救措施就是在每个具体案例中避免选择错误的目标函数——糟糕的目标函数反映了单个设计者的能力失败，而不是机器学习研究的主题。然而，上述例子表明，一个更有成效的视角可能是将错误的目标函数视为源于普遍原因（如部分观察到的目标），这些原因使得选择正确的目标变得具有挑战性。如果是这样，那么解决或缓解这些原因可能对安全性做出有价值的贡献。在这里，我们提出一些初步的、基于机器学习的方法来防止奖励黑客：

items.en: Adversarial Reward Functions: In some sense, the problem is that the ML system has an adversarial relationship with its reward function—it would like to find any way it can of exploiting problems in how the reward was specified to get high reward, whether or not its behavior corresponds to the intent of the reward specifier. In a typical setting, the machine learning system is a potentially powerful agent while the reward function is a static object that has no way of responding to the system’s attempts to game it. If instead the reward function were its own agent and could take actions to explore the environment, it might be much more difficult to fool. For instance, the reward agent could try to find scenarios that the ML system claimed were high reward but that a human labels as low reward; this is reminiscent of generative adversarial networks [61]. Of course, we would have to ensure that the reward-checking agent is more powerful (in a somewhat subtle sense) than the agent that is trying to achieve rewards. More generally, there may be interesting setups where a system has multiple pieces trained using different objectives that are used to check each other.
items.zh: 对抗性奖励函数：从某种意义上说，问题在于机器学习系统与其奖励函数之间存在对抗关系——它希望找到任何可以利用奖励函数规范中问题的方法来获得高奖励，无论其行为是否与奖励指定者的意图一致。在典型设置中，机器学习系统是一个潜在的强大智能体，而奖励函数是一个静态对象，无法对系统试图欺骗它的行为做出反应。如果奖励函数本身是一个智能体，并且可以采取行动探索环境，那么欺骗它就会困难得多。例如，奖励智能体可以尝试寻找机器学习系统声称是高奖励但人类标记为低奖励的场景；这让人联想到生成对抗网络[61]。当然，我们必须确保检查奖励的智能体比试图获得奖励的智能体更强大（在某种微妙的意义上）。更一般地说，可能存在有趣的设置，其中系统有多个部分，使用不同的目标进行训练，并相互检查。

items.en: Model Lookahead: In model based RL, the agent plans its future actions by using a model to consider which future states a sequence of actions may lead to. In some setups, we could give reward based on anticipated future states, rather than the present one. This could be very helpful in resisting situations where the model overwrites its reward function: you can’t control the reward once it replaces the reward function, but you can give negative reward for planning to replace the reward function. (Much like how a human would probably “enjoy” taking addictive substances once they do, but not want to be an addict.) Similar ideas are explored in [50, 71].
items.zh: 模型前瞻：在基于模型的强化学习中，智能体通过使用模型考虑一系列动作可能导致哪些未来状态来规划其未来行动。在某些设置中，我们可以基于预期的未来状态而不是当前状态给予奖励。这在抵抗模型覆盖其奖励函数的情况下可能非常有用：一旦它替换了奖励函数，你就无法控制奖励，但你可以为计划替换奖励函数的行为给予负奖励。（很像人类一旦服用成瘾物质可能会“享受”它，但不想成为瘾君子。）类似的想法在[50, 71]中进行了探讨。

items.en: Adversarial Blinding: Adversarial techniques can be used to blind a model to certain variables [5]. This technique could be used to make it impossible for an agent to understand some part of its environment, or even to have mutual information with it (or at least to penalize such mutual information). In particular, it could prevent an agent from understanding how its reward is generated, making it difficult to hack. This solution could be described as “cross-validation for agents.”
items.zh: 对抗性致盲：对抗性技术可用于使模型对某些变量失明[5]。这种技术可用于使智能体无法理解其环境的某些部分，甚至无法与之有互信息（或至少惩罚这种互信息）。特别是，它可以防止智能体理解其奖励是如何生成的，从而使其难以被黑客攻击。这种解决方案可以描述为“智能体的交叉验证”。

items.en: Careful Engineering: Some kinds of reward hacking, like the buffer overflow example, might be avoided by very careful engineering. In particular, formal verification or practical testing of parts of the system (perhaps facilitated by other machine learning systems) is likely to be valuable. Computer security approaches that attempt to isolate the agent from its reward signal through a sandbox could also be useful [17]. As with software engineering, we cannot expect this to catch every possible bug. It may be possible, however, to create some highly reliable “core” agent which could ensure reasonable behavior from the rest of the agent.
items.zh: 精心工程：某些类型的奖励黑客，如缓冲区溢出示例，可能通过非常仔细的工程来避免。特别是，对系统部分进行形式化验证或实际测试（可能由其他机器学习系统辅助）可能很有价值。试图通过沙箱将智能体与其奖励信号隔离的计算机安全方法也可能有用[17]。与软件工程一样，我们不能期望这能捕捉到每一个可能的错误。然而，有可能创建一些高度可靠的“核心”智能体，以确保智能体其余部分的合理行为。

items.en: Reward Capping: In some cases, simply capping the maximum possible reward may be an effective solution. However, while capping can prevent extreme low-probability, high-payoff strategies, it can’t prevent strategies like the cleaning robot closing its eyes to avoid seeing dirt. Also, the correct capping strategy could be subtle as we might need to cap total reward rather than reward per timestep.
items.zh: 奖励上限：在某些情况下，简单地限制最大可能奖励可能是一个有效的解决方案。然而，虽然上限可以防止极低概率、高回报的策略，但它无法防止像清洁机器人闭上眼睛以避免看到污垢这样的策略。此外，正确的上限策略可能很微妙，因为我们可能需要限制总奖励而不是每步奖励。

items.en: Counterexample Resistance: If we are worried, as in the case of abstract rewards, that learned components of our systems will be vulnerable to adversarial counterexamples, we can look to existing research in how to resist them, such as adversarial training [62]. Architectural decisions and weight uncertainty [26] may also help. Of course, adversarial counterexamples are just one manifestation of reward hacking, so counterexample resistance can only address a subset of these potential problems.
items.zh: 反例抵抗：如果我们担心，如抽象奖励的情况，我们系统的学习组件容易受到对抗性反例的攻击，我们可以参考现有的关于如何抵抗它们的研究，例如对抗性训练[62]。架构决策和权重不确定性[26]也可能有所帮助。当然，对抗性反例只是奖励黑客的一种表现形式，因此反例抵抗只能解决这些潜在问题的一个子集。

items.en: Multiple Rewards: A combination of multiple rewards [41] may be more difficult to hack and more robust. This could be different physical implementations of the same mathematical function, or different proxies for the same informal objective. We could combine reward functions by averaging, taking the minimum, taking quantiles, or something else entirely. Of course, there may still be bad behaviors which affect all the reward functions in a correlated manner.
items.zh: 多重奖励：多个奖励的组合[41]可能更难被黑客攻击，并且更加鲁棒。这可以是同一数学函数的不同物理实现，也可以是同一非正式目标的不同代理。我们可以通过平均、取最小值、取分位数或其他方式组合奖励函数。当然，仍然可能存在以相关方式影响所有奖励函数的糟糕行为。

items.en: Reward Pretraining: A possible defense against cases where the agent can influence its own reward function (e.g. feedback or environmental embedding) is to train a fixed reward function ahead of time as a supervised learning process divorced from interaction with the environment. This could involve either learning a reward function from samples of state-reward pairs, or from trajectories, as in inverse reinforcement learning [107, 51]. However, this forfeits the ability to further learn the reward function after the pretraining is complete, which may create other vulnerabilities.
items.zh: 奖励预训练：针对智能体可以影响其自身奖励函数（例如反馈或环境嵌入）的情况，一种可能的防御是提前训练一个固定的奖励函数，作为与环境交互分离的监督学习过程。这可能涉及从状态-奖励对样本中学习奖励函数，或者从轨迹中学习，如逆强化学习[107, 51]。然而，这放弃了在预训练完成后进一步学习奖励函数的能力，这可能会产生其他漏洞。

items.en: Variable Indifference: Often we want an agent to optimize certain variables in the environment, without trying to optimize others. For example, we might want an agent to maximize reward, without optimizing what the reward function is or trying to manipulate human behavior. Intuitively, we imagine a way to route the optimization pressure of powerful algorithms around parts of their environment. Truly solving this would have applications throughout safety—it seems connected to avoiding side effects and also to counterfactual reasoning. Of course, a challenge here is to make sure the variables targeted for indifference are actually the variables we care about in the world, as opposed to aliased or partially observed versions of them.
items.zh: 变量无感：通常我们希望智能体优化环境中的某些变量，而不试图优化其他变量。例如，我们可能希望智能体最大化奖励，而不优化奖励函数是什么或试图操纵人类行为。直观上，我们设想一种方法，将强大算法的优化压力绕过其环境的某些部分。真正解决这个问题将在整个安全性领域有应用——它似乎与避免副作用以及反事实推理有关。当然，这里的挑战是确保针对无感的变量实际上是我们在世界中关心的变量，而不是它们的别名或部分观察版本。

items.en: Trip Wires: If an agent is going to try and hack its reward function, it is preferable that we know this. We could deliberately introduce some plausible vulnerabilities (that an agent has the ability to exploit but should not exploit if its value function is correct) and monitor them, alerting us and stopping the agent immediately if it takes advantage of one. Such “trip wires” don’t solve reward hacking in itself, but may reduce the risk or at least provide diagnostics. Of course, with a sufficiently capable agent there is the risk that it could “see through” the trip wire and intentionally avoid it while still taking less obvious harmful actions.
items.zh: 绊网：如果智能体试图黑客攻击其奖励函数，我们最好知道这一点。我们可以故意引入一些看似合理的漏洞（智能体有能力利用，但如果其价值函数正确就不应该利用的漏洞），并监控它们，一旦智能体利用其中一个，就立即警告我们并停止智能体。这样的“绊网”本身并不能解决奖励黑客问题，但可能降低风险或至少提供诊断。当然，对于足够有能力的智能体，存在它可能“看穿”绊网并有意避开它，同时仍然采取不那么明显的有害行为的风险。

items.en: Fully solving this problem seems very difficult, but we believe the above approaches have the potential to ameliorate it, and might be scaled up or combined to yield more robust solutions. Given the predominantly theoretical focus on this problem to date, designing experiments that could induce the problem and test solutions might improve the relevance and clarity of this topic.
items.zh: 完全解决这个问题似乎非常困难，但我们相信上述方法有潜力改善它，并且可以扩展或组合以产生更鲁棒的解决方案。鉴于迄今为止对该问题的研究主要侧重于理论，设计能够引发该问题并测试解决方案的实验可能会提高该主题的相关性和清晰度。

items.en: Potential Experiments: A possible promising avenue of approach would be more realistic versions of the “delusion box” environment described by [127], in which standard RL agents distort their own perception to appear to receive high reward, rather than optimizing the objective in the external world that the reward signal was intended to encourage. The delusion box can be easily attached to any RL environment, but even more valuable would be to create classes of environments where a delusion box is a natural and integrated part of the dynamics. For example, in sufficiently rich physics simulations it is likely possible for an agent to alter the light waves in its immediate vicinity to distort its own perceptions. The goal would be to develop generalizable learning strategies that succeed at optimizing external objectives in a wide range of environments, while avoiding being fooled by delusion boxes that arise naturally in many diverse ways.
items.zh: 潜在实验：一个可能的有前景的方法是[127]描述的“妄想盒”环境的更现实版本，其中标准强化学习智能体扭曲自己的感知以显得获得高奖励，而不是优化奖励信号旨在鼓励的外部世界中的目标。妄想盒可以很容易地附加到任何强化学习环境中，但更有价值的是创建一类环境，其中妄想盒是动力学中自然且不可分割的一部分。例如，在足够丰富的物理模拟中，智能体很可能可以改变其附近的光波以扭曲自己的感知。目标是开发可泛化的学习策略，在广泛的环境中成功优化外部目标，同时避免被以多种不同方式自然出现的妄想盒所欺骗。

items.en: Consider an autonomous agent performing some complex task, such as cleaning an office in the case of our recurring robot example. We may want the agent to maximize a complex objective like “if the user spent a few hours looking at the result in detail, how happy would they be with the agent’s performance?” But we don’t have enough time to provide such oversight for every training example; in order to actually train the agent, we need to rely on cheaper approximations, like “does the user seem happy when they see the office?” or “is there any visible dirt on the floor?” These cheaper signals can be efficiently evaluated during training, but they don’t perfectly track what we care about. This divergence exacerbates problems like unintended side effects (which may be appropriately penalized by the complex objective but omitted from the cheap approximation) and reward hacking (which thorough oversight might recognize as undesirable). We may be able to ameliorate such problems by finding more efficient ways to exploit our limited oversight budget—for example by combining limited calls to the true objective function with frequent calls to an imperfect proxy that we are given or can learn.
items.zh: 考虑一个执行复杂任务的自主智能体，例如我们反复提到的机器人清理办公室的例子。我们可能希望智能体最大化一个复杂的目标，比如“如果用户花几个小时详细查看结果，他们对智能体的表现会有多满意？”但我们没有足够的时间为每个训练样本提供这样的监督；为了实际训练智能体，我们需要依赖更廉价的近似，比如“用户看到办公室时是否显得高兴？”或“地板上是否有可见的污垢？”这些更廉价的信号可以在训练期间高效评估，但它们并不能完美地追踪我们真正关心的东西。这种差异会加剧意外副作用（复杂目标可能会适当惩罚，但廉价近似可能忽略）和奖励破解（彻底的监督可能识别出不良行为）等问题。我们或许可以通过找到更有效的方法来利用有限的监督预算来改善这些问题——例如，将有限次调用真实目标函数与频繁调用我们已知或可以学习的不完美代理相结合。

items.en: One framework for thinking about this problem is semi-supervised reinforcement learning,333The discussion of semi-supervised RL draws heavily on an informal essay, https://medium.com/ai-control/cf7d5375197f written by one of the authors of the present document. which resembles ordinary reinforcement learning except that the agent can only see its reward on a small fraction of the timesteps or episodes. The agent’s performance is still evaluated based on reward from all episodes but it must optimize this based only on the limited reward samples it sees.
items.zh: 思考这个问题的一个框架是半监督强化学习，它与普通强化学习类似，不同之处在于智能体只能在很小一部分时间步或回合中看到奖励。智能体的性能仍然基于所有回合的奖励来评估，但它必须仅基于它看到的有限奖励样本来优化。

items.en: The active learning setting seems most interesting; in this setting the agent can request to see the reward on whatever episodes or timesteps would be most useful for learning, and the goal is to be economical both with number of feedback requests and total training time. We can also consider a random setting, where the reward is visible on a random subset of the timesteps or episodes, as well as intermediate possibilities.
items.zh: 主动学习设置似乎最有趣；在这种设置中，智能体可以请求查看对学习最有用的回合或时间步的奖励，目标是既节省反馈请求次数又节省总训练时间。我们还可以考虑随机设置，其中奖励在随机子集的时间步或回合上可见，以及介于两者之间的可能性。

items.en: We can define a baseline performance by simply ignoring the unlabeled episodes and applying an ordinary RL algorithm to the labelled episodes. This will generally result in very slow learning. The challenge is to make use of the unlabelled episodes to accelerate learning, ideally learning almost as quickly and robustly as if all episodes had been labeled.
items.zh: 我们可以通过简单地忽略未标记的回合，并将普通强化学习算法应用于标记的回合来定义基线性能。这通常会导致学习非常缓慢。挑战在于利用未标记的回合来加速学习，理想情况下学习速度和鲁棒性几乎与所有回合都被标记时一样。

items.en: An important subtask of semi-supervised RL is identifying proxies which predict the reward, and learning the conditions under which those proxies are valid. For example, if a cleaning robot’s real reward is given by a detailed human evaluation, then it could learn that asking the human “is the room clean?” can provide a very useful approximation to the reward function, and it could eventually learn that checking for visible dirt is an even cheaper but still-useful approximation. This could allow it to learn a good cleaning policy using an extremely small number of detailed evaluations.
items.zh: 半监督强化学习的一个重要子任务是识别预测奖励的代理，并学习这些代理有效的条件。例如，如果清洁机器人的真实奖励由详细的人类评估给出，那么它可以学习询问人类“房间干净吗？”可以提供对奖励函数非常有用的近似，并且它最终可以学习检查可见污垢是一个更便宜但仍然有用的近似。这可以使其使用极少量的详细评估来学习良好的清洁策略。

items.en: More broadly, use of semi-supervised RL with a reliable but sparse true approval metric may incentivize communication and transparency by the agent, since the agent will want to get as much cheap proxy feedback as it possibly can about whether its decisions will ultimately be given high reward. For example, hiding a mess under the rug simply breaks the correspondence between the user’s reaction and the real reward signal, and so would be avoided.
items.zh: 更广泛地说，使用具有可靠但稀疏的真实批准度量的半监督强化学习可能会激励智能体的沟通和透明度，因为智能体希望获得尽可能多的廉价代理反馈，以了解其决策最终是否会获得高奖励。例如，将混乱藏在地毯下只会破坏用户反应与真实奖励信号之间的对应关系，因此会被避免。

items.en: We can imagine many possible approaches to semi-supervised RL. For example:
items.zh: 我们可以想象许多可能的半监督强化学习方法。例如：

items.en: Supervised Reward Learning: Train a model to predict the reward from the state on either a per-timestep or per-episode basis, and use it to estimate the payoff of unlabelled episodes, with some appropriate weighting or uncertainty estimate to account for lower confidence in estimated vs known reward. [37] studies a version of this with direct human feedback as the reward. Many existing RL approaches already fit estimators that closely resemble reward predictors (especially policy gradient methods with a strong baseline, see e.g. [134]), suggesting that this approach may be eminently feasible.
items.zh: 监督奖励学习：训练一个模型，在每个时间步或每个回合的基础上从状态预测奖励，并用它来估计未标记回合的回报，并采用适当的权重或不确定性估计来解释估计奖励与已知奖励相比置信度较低的问题。[37] 研究了以直接人类反馈作为奖励的版本。许多现有的强化学习方法已经使用了与奖励预测器非常相似的估计器（特别是具有强基线的策略梯度方法，参见例如 [134]），这表明这种方法可能非常可行。

items.en: Semi-supervised or Active Reward Learning: Combine the above with traditional semi-supervised or active learning, to more quickly learn the reward estimator. For example, the agent could learn to identify “salient” events in the environment, and request to see the reward associated with these events.
items.zh: 半监督或主动奖励学习：将上述方法与传统的半监督或主动学习相结合，以更快地学习奖励估计器。例如，智能体可以学习识别环境中的“显著”事件，并请求查看与这些事件相关的奖励。

items.en: Unsupervised Value Iteration: Use the observed transitions of the unlabeled episodes to make more accurate Bellman updates.
items.zh: 无监督价值迭代：利用未标记回合中观察到的转移进行更准确的贝尔曼更新。

items.en: Unsupervised Model Learning: If using model-based RL, use the observed transitions of the unlabeled episodes to improve the quality of the model.
items.zh: 无监督模型学习：如果使用基于模型的强化学习，利用未标记回合中观察到的转移来提高模型质量。

items.en: As a toy example, a semi-supervised RL agent should be able to learn to play Atari games using a small number of direct reward signals, relying almost entirely on the visual display of the score. This simple example can be extended to capture other safety issues: for example, the agent might have the ability to modify the displayed score without modifying the real score, or the agent may need to take some special action (such as pausing the game) in order to see its score, or the agent may need to learn a sequence of increasingly rough-and-ready approximations (for example learning that certain sounds are associated with positive rewards and other sounds with negative rewards). Or, even without the visual display of the score, the agent might be able to learn to play from only a handful of explicit reward requests (“how many points did I get on the frame where that enemy ship blew up? How about the bigger enemy ship?”)
items.zh: 作为一个玩具示例，半监督强化学习智能体应该能够使用少量直接奖励信号学习玩 Atari 游戏，几乎完全依赖于分数的视觉显示。这个简单的例子可以扩展到捕捉其他安全问题：例如，智能体可能能够修改显示的分数而不修改真实分数，或者智能体可能需要采取一些特殊动作（例如暂停游戏）才能看到其分数，或者智能体可能需要学习一系列越来越粗略的近似（例如学习某些声音与正奖励相关，其他声音与负奖励相关）。或者，即使没有分数的视觉显示，智能体也可能仅通过少量明确的奖励请求（“在那艘敌舰爆炸的帧上我得了多少分？那艘更大的敌舰呢？”）来学习玩游戏。

items.en: An effective approach to semi-supervised RL might be a strong first step towards providing scalable oversight and mitigating other AI safety problems. It would also likely be useful for reinforcement learning, independent of its relevance to safety.
items.zh: 有效的半监督强化学习方法可能是迈向提供可扩展监督和缓解其他人工智能安全问题的重要第一步。它也可能对强化学习本身有用，无论其与安全的相关性如何。

items.en: There are other possible approaches to scalable oversight:
items.zh: 还有其他可能的方法来实现可扩展监督：

items.en: Distant supervision. Rather than providing evaluations of some small fraction of a system’s decisions, we could provide some useful information about the system’s decisions in the aggregate or some noisy hints about the correct evaluations There has been some work in this direction within the area of semi-supervised or weakly supervised learning. For instance, generalized expectation criteria [94, 45] ask the user to provide population-level statistics (e.g. telling the system that on average each sentence contains at least one noun); the DeepDive system [139] asks users to supply rules that each generate many weak labels; and [65] extrapolates more general patterns from an initial set of low-recall labeling rules. This general approach is often referred to as distant supervision, and has also received recent attention in the natural language processing community (see e.g. [60, 99] as well as several of the references above). Expanding these lines of work and finding a way to apply them to the case of agents, where feedback is more interactive and i.i.d. assumptions may be violated, could provide an approach to scalable oversight that is complementary to the approach embodied in semi-supervised RL.
items.zh: 远程监督。我们不是提供对系统一小部分决策的评估，而是提供关于系统决策的总体有用信息或关于正确评估的一些噪声提示。在半监督或弱监督学习领域已经有一些这方面的工作。例如，广义期望准则 [94, 45] 要求用户提供总体统计信息（例如告诉系统每个句子平均至少包含一个名词）；DeepDive 系统 [139] 要求用户提供每个生成许多弱标签的规则；[65] 从一组初始的低召回率标签规则中推断出更一般的模式。这种通用方法通常被称为远程监督，最近在自然语言处理社区也受到了关注（参见例如 [60, 99] 以及上述一些参考文献）。扩展这些工作线并找到将其应用于智能体情况的方法，其中反馈更具交互性且 i.i.d. 假设可能被违反，可能提供一种与半监督强化学习所体现的方法互补的可扩展监督方法。

items.en: Hierarchical reinforcement learning. Hierarchical reinforcement learning [40] offers another approach to scalable oversight. Here a top-level agent takes a relatively small number of highly abstract actions that extend over large temporal or spatial scales, and receives rewards over similarly long timescales. The agent completes actions by delegating them to sub-agents, which it incentivizes with a synthetic reward signal representing correct completion of the action, and which themselves delegate to sub-sub-agents. At the lowest level, agents directly take primitive actions in the environment.
items.zh: 分层强化学习。分层强化学习 [40] 提供了另一种可扩展监督的方法。在这里，顶层智能体采取相对较少的高度抽象的动作，这些动作跨越大的时间或空间尺度，并在类似长的时间尺度上接收奖励。智能体通过将动作委托给子智能体来完成动作，它通过代表正确完成动作的合成奖励信号来激励子智能体，而子智能体又委托给子-子智能体。在最底层，智能体直接在环境中采取原始动作。

items.en: The top-level agent in hierarchical RL may be able to learn from very sparse rewards, since it does not need to learn how to implement the details of its policy; meanwhile, the sub-agents will receive a dense reward signal even if the top-level reward is very sparse, since they are optimizing synthetic reward signals defined by higher-level agents. So a successful approach to hierarchical RL might naturally facilitate scalable oversight.444When implementing hierarchical RL, we may find that subagents take actions that don’t serve top-level agent’s real goals, in the same way that a human may be concerned that the top-level agent’s actions don’t serve the human’s real goals. This is an intriguing analogy that suggests that there may be fruitful parallels between hierarchical RL and several aspects of the safety problem.
items.zh: 分层强化学习中的顶层智能体可能能够从非常稀疏的奖励中学习，因为它不需要学习如何实现其策略的细节；同时，子智能体将收到密集的奖励信号，即使顶层奖励非常稀疏，因为它们正在优化由更高级智能体定义的合成奖励信号。因此，成功的分层强化学习方法可能自然地促进可扩展监督。当实现分层强化学习时，我们可能会发现子智能体采取的行动并不服务于顶层智能体的真正目标，就像人类可能担心顶层智能体的行动不服务于人类的真正目标一样。这是一个有趣的类比，表明分层强化学习与安全问题的几个方面之间可能存在富有成效的相似之处。

items.en: Hierarchical RL seems a particularly promising approach to oversight, especially given the potential promise of combining ideas from hierarchical RL with neural network function approximators [84].
items.zh: 分层强化学习似乎是一种特别有前途的监督方法，特别是考虑到将分层强化学习的思想与神经网络函数逼近器相结合 [84] 的潜在前景。

items.en: Potential Experiments: An extremely simple experiment would be to try semi-supervised RL in some basic control environments, such as cartpole balance or pendulum swing-up. If the reward is provided only on a random 10% of episodes, can we still learn nearly as quickly as if it were provided every episode? In such tasks the reward structure is very simple so success should be quite likely. A next step would be to try the same on Atari games. Here the active learning case could be quite interesting—perhaps it is possible to infer the reward structure from just a few carefully requested samples (for example, frames where enemy ships are blowing up in Space Invaders), and thus learn to play the games in an almost totally unsupervised fashion. The next step after this might be to try a task with much more complex reward structure, either simulated or (preferably) real-world. If learning was sufficiently data-efficient, then these rewards could be provided directly by a human. Robot locomotion or industrial control tasks might be a natural candidate for such experiments.
items.zh: 潜在实验：一个极其简单的实验是在一些基本的控制环境中尝试半监督强化学习，例如倒立摆平衡或摆锤摆动。如果奖励仅在随机 10% 的回合中提供，我们能否仍然几乎像每个回合都提供奖励一样快地学习？在这些任务中，奖励结构非常简单，因此成功应该相当可能。下一步是在 Atari 游戏上尝试同样的方法。在这里，主动学习案例可能非常有趣——也许可以从几个精心请求的样本（例如，太空入侵者中敌舰爆炸的帧）中推断出奖励结构，从而以几乎完全无监督的方式学习玩游戏。这之后的下一步可能是在具有更复杂奖励结构的任务上尝试，无论是模拟的还是（最好）真实世界的。如果学习足够数据高效，那么这些奖励可以直接由人类提供。机器人运动或工业控制任务可能是此类实验的自然候选。

items.en: All autonomous learning agents need to sometimes engage in exploration—taking actions that don’t seem ideal given current information, but which help the agent learn about its environment. However, exploration can be dangerous, since it involves taking actions whose consequences the agent doesn’t understand well. In toy environments, like an Atari video game, there’s a limit to how bad these consequences can be—maybe the agent loses some score, or runs into an enemy and suffers some damage. But the real world can be much less forgiving. Badly chosen actions may destroy the agent or trap it in states it can’t get out of. Robot helicopters may run into the ground or damage property; industrial control systems could cause serious issues. Common exploration policies such as epsilon-greedy [150] or R-max [31] explore by choosing an action at random or viewing unexplored actions optimistically, and thus make no attempt to avoid these dangerous situations. More sophisticated exploration strategies that adopt a coherent exploration policy over extended temporal scales [114] could actually have even greater potential for harm, since a coherently chosen bad policy may be more insidious than mere random actions. Yet intuitively it seems like it should often be possible to predict which actions are dangerous and explore in a way that avoids them, even when we don’t have that much information about the environment. For example, if I want to learn about tigers, should I buy a tiger, or buy a book about tigers? It takes only a tiny bit of prior knowledge about tigers to determine which option is safer.
items.zh: 所有自主学习智能体有时都需要进行探索——即采取那些根据当前信息看似不理想，但能帮助智能体了解其环境的行动。然而，探索可能很危险，因为它涉及采取智能体尚不完全了解其后果的行动。在玩具环境（如Atari视频游戏）中，这些后果的严重程度是有限的——智能体可能损失一些分数，或撞上敌人并受到一些伤害。但现实世界可能远没有那么宽容。选择不当的行动可能会摧毁智能体，或将其困在无法脱身的状态中。机器人直升机可能撞到地面或损坏财产；工业控制系统可能引发严重问题。常见的探索策略，如ε-贪婪[150]或R-max[31]，通过随机选择行动或乐观地看待未探索的行动来进行探索，因此并未试图避免这些危险情况。更复杂的探索策略在长时间尺度上采用连贯的探索策略[114]，实际上可能造成更大的危害，因为连贯选择的糟糕策略可能比单纯的随机行动更具隐蔽性。然而，直觉上，我们似乎常常能够预测哪些行动是危险的，并以一种避免危险的方式进行探索，即使我们对环境了解不多。例如，如果我想了解老虎，我应该买一只老虎，还是买一本关于老虎的书？只需要一点点关于老虎的先验知识，就能判断哪个选项更安全。

items.en: In practice, real world RL projects can often avoid these issues by simply hard-coding an avoidance of catastrophic behaviors. For instance, an RL-based robot helicopter might be programmed to override its policy with a hard-coded collision avoidance sequence (such as spinning its propellers to gain altitude) whenever it’s too close to the ground. This approach works well when there are only a few things that could go wrong, and the designers know all of them ahead of time. But as agents become more autonomous and act in more complex domains, it may become harder and harder to anticipate every possible catastrophic failure. The space of failure modes for an agent running a power grid or a search-and-rescue operation could be quite large. Hard-coding against every possible failure is unlikely to be feasible in these cases, so a more principled approach to preventing harmful exploration seems essential. Even in simple cases like the robot helicopter, a principled approach would simplify system design and reduce the need for domain-specific engineering.
items.zh: 在实践中，现实世界的强化学习项目通常可以通过简单地硬编码来避免灾难性行为，从而规避这些问题。例如，基于强化学习的机器人直升机可能会被编程为，每当它离地面太近时，就用硬编码的碰撞避免序列（例如旋转螺旋桨以升高高度）来覆盖其策略。当只有少数几件事可能出错，并且设计者事先知道所有这些情况时，这种方法效果很好。但随着智能体变得更加自主，并在更复杂的领域中行动，预测每一种可能的灾难性失败可能会变得越来越困难。运行电网或搜救行动的智能体的失败模式空间可能相当大。在这些情况下，针对每一种可能的失败进行硬编码不太可行，因此，一种更有原则性的防止有害探索的方法似乎至关重要。即使在像机器人直升机这样的简单案例中，一种有原则性的方法也能简化系统设计，并减少对特定领域工程的需求。

items.en: There is a sizable literature on such safe exploration—it is arguably the most studied of the problems we discuss in this document. [55, 118] provide thorough reviews of this literature, so we don’t review it extensively here, but simply describe some general routes that this research has taken, as well as suggesting some directions that might have increasing relevance as RL systems expand in scope and capability.
items.zh: 关于这种安全探索的文献相当丰富——可以说，它是本文讨论的问题中研究最多的。[55, 118]对这些文献进行了全面综述，因此我们在此不做广泛回顾，仅简要描述这项研究的一些一般路径，并提出一些随着强化学习系统在范围和能力上的扩展可能越来越具有相关性的方向。

items.en: Risk-Sensitive Performance Criteria: A body of existing literature considers changing the optimization criteria from expected total reward to other objectives that are better at preventing rare, catastrophic events; see [55] for a thorough and up-to-date review of this literature. These approaches involve optimizing worst-case performance, or ensuring that the probability of very bad performance is small, or penalizing the variance in performance. These methods have not yet been tested with expressive function approximators such as deep neural networks, but this should be possible in principle for some of the methods, such as [153], which proposes a modification to policy gradient algorithms to optimize a risk-sensitive criterion. There is also recent work studying how to estimate uncertainty in value functions that are represented by deep neural networks [114, 53]; these ideas could be incorporated into risk-sensitive RL algorithms. Another line of work relevant to risk sensitivity uses off-policy estimation to perform a policy update that is good with high probability [156].
items.zh: 风险敏感性能标准：现有文献中有一部分考虑将优化标准从期望总回报改为其他更能防止罕见灾难性事件的目标；参见[55]对该文献的全面最新综述。这些方法涉及优化最坏情况性能，或确保极差性能的概率很小，或对性能的方差进行惩罚。这些方法尚未使用深度神经网络等表达能力强的函数逼近器进行测试，但对于某些方法，如[153]，原则上应该是可行的，该文提出对策略梯度算法进行修改以优化风险敏感标准。最近还有研究如何估计由深度神经网络表示的值函数中的不确定性[114, 53]；这些想法可以纳入风险敏感的强化学习算法中。另一条与风险敏感性相关的工作线使用离策略估计来执行一个以高概率为好的策略更新[156]。

items.en: Use Demonstrations: Exploration is necessary to ensure that the agent finds the states that are necessary for near-optimal performance. We may be able to avoid the need for exploration altogether if we instead use inverse RL or apprenticeship learning, where the learning algorithm is provided with expert trajectories of near-optimal behavior [128, 2]. Recent progress in inverse reinforcement learning using deep neural networks to learn the cost function or policy [51] suggests that it might also be possible to reduce the need for exploration in advanced RL systems by training on a small set of demonstrations. Such demonstrations could be used to create a baseline policy, such that even if further learning is necessary, exploration away from the baseline policy can be limited in magnitude.
items.zh: 使用示范：探索对于确保智能体找到接近最优性能所需的状态是必要的。如果我们改用逆强化学习或学徒学习，其中学习算法被提供接近最优行为的专家轨迹[128, 2]，我们或许可以完全避免探索的需要。最近在使用深度神经网络学习成本函数或策略的逆强化学习方面的进展[51]表明，通过在一小组示范上进行训练，也可能减少高级强化学习系统中探索的需求。这样的示范可用于创建基线策略，这样即使需要进一步学习，偏离基线策略的探索也可以在幅度上受到限制。

items.en: Simulated Exploration: The more we can do our exploration in simulated environments instead of the real world, the less opportunity there is for catastrophe. It will probably always be necessary to do some real-world exploration, since many complex situations cannot be perfectly captured by a simulator, but it might be possible to learn about danger in simulation and then adopt a more conservative “safe exploration” policy when acting in the real world. Training RL agents (particularly robots) in simulated environments is already quite common, so advances in “exploration-focused simulation” could be easily incorporated into current workflows. In systems that involve a continual cycle of learning and deployment, there may be interesting research problems associated with how to safely incrementally update policies given simulation-based trajectories that imperfectly represent the consequences of those policies as well as reliably accurate off-policy trajectories (e.g. “semi-on-policy” evaluation).
items.zh: 模拟探索：我们越能在模拟环境而非现实世界中进行探索，灾难发生的机会就越少。可能总是需要一些现实世界的探索，因为许多复杂情况无法被模拟器完美捕捉，但有可能在模拟中学习危险，然后在现实世界中采取更保守的“安全探索”策略。在模拟环境中训练强化学习智能体（尤其是机器人）已经相当普遍，因此“以探索为中心的模拟”的进步可以很容易地融入当前的工作流程。在涉及学习和部署持续循环的系统中，可能存在有趣的研究问题，涉及如何根据模拟轨迹（这些轨迹不完美地代表策略的后果）以及可靠准确的离策略轨迹（例如“半在策略”评估）来安全地增量更新策略。

items.en: Bounded Exploration: If we know that a certain portion of state space is safe, and that even the worst action within it can be recovered from or bounded in harm, we can allow the agent to run freely within those bounds. For example, a quadcopter sufficiently far from the ground might be able to explore safely, since even if something goes wrong there will be ample time for a human or another policy to rescue it. Better yet, if we have a model, we can extrapolate forward and ask whether an action will take us outside the safe state space.
