Safety can be defined as remaining within an ergodic region of the state space such that actions are reversible [104, 159], or as limiting the probability of huge negative reward to some small value [156]. Yet another approaches uses separate safety and performance functions and attempts to obey constraints on the safety function with high probabilty [22]. As with several of the other directions, applying or adapting these methods to recently developed advanced RL systems could be a promising area of research. This idea seems related to H-infinity control [20] and regional verification [148].
items.zh: 有界探索：如果我们知道状态空间的某一部分是安全的，并且即使其中的最坏行动也可以从中恢复或危害有限，我们就可以允许智能体在这些边界内自由运行。例如，一架离地面足够远的四轴飞行器可能能够安全探索，因为即使出现问题，也有足够的时间让人类或其他策略来救援它。更好的是，如果我们有一个模型，我们可以向前外推，询问某个行动是否会将我们带出安全状态空间。安全性可以定义为保持在状态空间的遍历区域内，使得行动是可逆的[104, 159]，或者将巨大负奖励的概率限制在某个小值[156]。另一种方法使用独立的安全和性能函数，并试图以高概率遵守安全函数的约束[22]。与其他几个方向一样，将这些方法应用于或适应于最近开发的高级强化学习系统可能是一个有前景的研究领域。这个想法似乎与H无穷控制[20]和区域验证[148]有关。

items.en: Trusted Policy Oversight: If we have a trusted policy and a model of the environment, we can limit exploration to actions the trusted policy believes we can recover from. It’s fine to dive towards the ground, as long as we know we can pull out of the dive in time.
items.zh: 可信策略监督：如果我们有一个可信策略和一个环境模型，我们可以将探索限制在可信策略认为我们可以从中恢复的行动上。向地面俯冲是可以的，只要我们知道我们能及时拉起。

items.en: Human Oversight: Another possibility is to check potentially unsafe actions with a human. Unfortunately, this problem runs into the scalable oversight problem: the agent may need to make too many exploratory actions for human oversight to be practical, or may need to make them too fast for humans to judge them. A key challenge to making this work is having the agent be a good judge of which exploratory actions are genuinely risky, versus which are safe actions it can unilaterally take; another challenge is finding appropriately safe actions to take while waiting for the oversight.
items.zh: 人类监督：另一种可能性是让人类检查潜在不安全的行动。不幸的是，这个问题遇到了可扩展监督问题：智能体可能需要执行过多的探索性行动，使得人类监督不切实际，或者可能需要以人类无法判断的速度执行这些行动。使这种方法可行的关键挑战是让智能体能够良好地判断哪些探索性行动是真正有风险的，哪些是它可以单方面采取的安全行动；另一个挑战是在等待监督的同时找到适当安全的行动。

items.en: Potential Experiments: It might be helpful to have a suite of toy environments where unwary agents can fall prey to harmful exploration, but there is enough pattern to the possible catastrophes that clever agents can predict and avoid them. To some extent this feature already exists in autonomous helicopter competitions and Mars rover simulations [104], but there is always the risk of catastrophes being idiosyncratic, such that trained agents can overfit to them. A truly broad set of environments, containing conceptually distinct pitfalls that can cause unwary agents to receive extremely negative reward, and covering both physical and abstract catastrophes, might help in the development of safe exploration techniques for advanced RL systems. Such a suite of environments might serve a benchmarking role similar to that of the bAbI tasks [163], with the eventual goal being to develop a single architecture that can learn to avoid catastrophes in all environments in the suite.
items.zh: 潜在实验：拥有一套玩具环境可能会有所帮助，在这些环境中，不谨慎的智能体可能会陷入有害探索，但可能的灾难有足够的模式，使得聪明的智能体能够预测并避免它们。在某种程度上，这种特征已经存在于自主直升机竞赛和火星车模拟中[104]，但总是存在灾难是特异的风险，使得训练过的智能体可能过拟合它们。一套真正广泛的环境，包含概念上不同的陷阱，这些陷阱可能导致不谨慎的智能体获得极其负面的奖励，并涵盖物理和抽象的灾难，可能有助于开发高级强化学习系统的安全探索技术。这样一套环境可能起到类似于bAbI任务[163]的基准测试作用，最终目标是开发一个单一的架构，能够学会避免该套件中所有环境中的灾难。

items.en: All of us occasionally find ourselves in situations that our previous experience has not adequately prepared us to deal with—for instance, flying an airplane, traveling to a country whose culture is very different from ours, or taking care of children for the first time. Such situations are inherently difficult to handle and inevitably lead to some missteps. However, a key (and often rare) skill in dealing with such situations is to recognize our own ignorance, rather than simply assuming that the heuristics and intuitions we’ve developed for other situations will carry over perfectly. Machine learning systems also have this problem—a speech system trained on clean speech will perform very poorly on noisy speech, yet often be highly confident in its erroneous classifications (some of the authors have personally observed this in training automatic speech recognition systems). In the case of our cleaning robot, harsh cleaning materials that it has found useful in cleaning factory floors could cause a lot of harm if used to clean an office. Or, an office might contain pets that the robot, never having seen before, attempts to wash with soap, leading to predictably bad results. In general, when the testing distribution differs from the training distribution, machine learning systems may not only exhibit poor performance, but also wrongly assume that their performance is good.
items.zh: 我们所有人偶尔都会发现自己处于以往经验未能充分准备应对的境地——例如，驾驶飞机、前往文化迥异的国度旅行，或是初次照顾孩子。这类情况本就难以处理，且不可避免地会导致一些失误。然而，应对此类情况的一个关键（且往往罕见的）技能是认识到自己的无知，而非简单假设为其他情境发展出的启发式方法和直觉能完美迁移。机器学习系统也存在这个问题——在干净语音上训练的语音系统在嘈杂语音上表现极差，却往往对其错误分类高度自信（部分作者在训练自动语音识别系统时曾亲身观察到这一点）。以我们的清洁机器人为例，它在清洁工厂地板时发现有用的强力清洁材料，若用于清洁办公室则可能造成巨大损害。或者，办公室中可能含有宠物，机器人从未见过，却试图用肥皂清洗，导致可预见的糟糕结果。一般而言，当测试分布与训练分布不同时，机器学习系统不仅可能表现不佳，还可能错误地认为其性能良好。

items.en: Such errors can be harmful or offensive—a classifier could give the wrong medical diagnosis with such high confidence that the data isn’t flagged for human inspection, or a language model could output offensive text that it confidently believes is non-problematic. For autonomous agents acting in the world, there may be even greater potential for something bad to happen—for instance, an autonomous agent might overload a power grid because it incorrectly but confidently perceives that a particular region doesn’t have enough power, and concludes that more power is urgently needed and overload is unlikely. More broadly, any agent whose perception or heuristic reasoning processes are not trained on the correct distribution may badly misunderstand its situation, and thus runs the risk of committing harmful actions that it does not realize are harmful. Additionally, safety checks that depend on trained machine learning systems (e.g. “does my visual system believe this route is clear?”) may fail silently and unpredictably if those systems encounter real-world data that differs sufficiently from their training data. Having a better way to detect such failures, and ultimately having statistical assurances about how often they’ll happen, seems critical to building safe and predictable systems.
items.zh: 此类错误可能有害或具有冒犯性——分类器可能以极高置信度给出错误医疗诊断，以至于数据未被标记供人工检查；或语言模型可能输出其自信认为无问题的冒犯性文本。对于在现实世界中行动的自主智能体，发生不良事件的可能性甚至更大——例如，自主智能体可能使电网过载，因为它错误但自信地认为某个特定区域电力不足，并断定急需更多电力且过载不可能发生。更广泛地说，任何感知或启发式推理过程未在正确分布上训练的智能体都可能严重误解其处境，从而有实施其未意识到的有害行为的风险。此外，依赖于训练好的机器学习系统的安全检查（例如“我的视觉系统认为这条路线畅通吗？”）可能会在那些系统遇到与训练数据差异足够大的真实世界数据时，无声且不可预测地失效。拥有更好的方法来检测此类失败，并最终对其发生频率有统计保证，对于构建安全且可预测的系统似乎至关重要。

items.en: For concreteness, we imagine that a machine learning model is trained on one distribution (call it p0subscript𝑝0p_{0}) but deployed on a potentially different test distribution (call it p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}). There are many other ways to formalize this problem (for instance, in an online learning setting with concept drift [70, 54]) but we will focus on the above for simplicity. An important point is that we likely have access to a large amount of labeled data at training time, but little or no labeled data at test time. Our goal is to ensure that the model “performs reasonably” on p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}, in the sense that (1) it often performs well on p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}, and (2) it knows when it is performing badly (and ideally can avoid/mitigate the bad performance by taking conservative actions or soliciting human input).
items.zh: 具体而言，我们设想一个机器学习模型在一个分布（记为 p0）上训练，但在一个可能不同的测试分布（记为 p*）上部署。有许多其他方式形式化此问题（例如，在具有概念漂移的在线学习设置中 [70, 54]），但为简单起见，我们将聚焦于上述形式。一个重要点是，我们在训练时可能拥有大量标记数据，但在测试时很少或没有标记数据。我们的目标是确保模型在 p* 上“表现合理”，即 (1) 它在 p* 上通常表现良好，且 (2) 它知道自己何时表现不佳（并理想情况下能通过采取保守行动或征求人类输入来避免/减轻不良表现）。

items.en: There are a variety of areas that are potentially relevant to this problem, including change detection and anomaly detection [21, 80, 91], hypothesis testing [145], transfer learning [138, 124, 125, 25], and several others [136, 87, 18, 122, 121, 74, 147]. Rather than fully reviewing all of this work in detail (which would necessitate a paper in itself), we will describe a few illustrative approaches and lay out some of their relative strengths and challenges.
items.zh: 有多个领域可能与这个问题相关，包括变化检测和异常检测 [21, 80, 91]、假设检验 [145]、迁移学习 [138, 124, 125, 25] 以及其他一些领域 [136, 87, 18, 122, 121, 74, 147]。我们不会详细回顾所有这些工作（这本身就需要一篇论文），而是描述一些有代表性的方法，并阐述它们的一些相对优势和挑战。

items.en: Well-specified models: covariate shift and marginal likelihood. If we specialize to prediction tasks and let x𝑥x denote the input and y𝑦y denote the output (prediction target), then one possibility is to make the covariate shift assumption that p0​(y|x)=p∗​(y|x)subscript𝑝0conditional𝑦𝑥superscript𝑝conditional𝑦𝑥p_{0}(y|x)=p^{*}(y|x). In this case, assuming that we can model p0​(x)subscript𝑝0𝑥p_{0}(x) and p∗​(x)superscript𝑝𝑥p^{*}(x) well, we can perform importance weighting by re-weighting each training example (x,y)𝑥𝑦(x,y) by p∗​(x)/p0​(x)superscript𝑝𝑥subscript𝑝0𝑥p^{*}(x)/p_{0}(x) [138, 124]. Then the importance-weighted samples allow us to estimate the performance on p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}, and even re-train a model to perform well on p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}. This approach is limited by the variance of the importance estimate, which is very large or even infinite unless p0subscript𝑝0p_{0} and p∗superscript𝑝p^{*} are close together.
items.zh: 良好指定的模型：协变量偏移和边际似然。如果我们专注于预测任务，令 x 表示输入，y 表示输出（预测目标），那么一种可能性是做出协变量偏移假设，即 p0(y|x) = p*(y|x)。在这种情况下，假设我们能很好地建模 p0(x) 和 p*(x)，我们可以通过将每个训练样本 (x,y) 按 p*(x)/p0(x) 重新加权来执行重要性加权 [138, 124]。然后，重要性加权样本使我们能够估计在 p* 上的性能，甚至重新训练一个模型以在 p* 上表现良好。此方法受限于重要性估计的方差，除非 p0 和 p* 接近，否则方差非常大甚至无限。

items.en: An alternative to sample re-weighting involves assuming a well-specified model family, in which case there is a single optimal model for predicting under both p0subscript𝑝0p_{0} and p∗superscript𝑝p^{*}. In this case, one need only heed finite-sample variance in the estimated model [25, 87]. A limitation to this approach, at least currently, is that models are often mis-specified in practice. However, this could potentially be overcome by employing highly expressive model families such as reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces [72], Turing machines [143, 144], or sufficiently expressive neural nets [64, 79]. In the latter case, there has been interesting recent work on using bootstrapping to estimate finite-sample variation in the learned parameters of a neural network [114]; it seems worthwhile to better understand whether this approach can be used to effectively estimate out-of-sample performance in practice, as well as how local minima, lack of curvature, and other peculiarities relative to the typical setting of the bootstrap [47] affect the validity of this approach.
items.zh: 样本重新加权的替代方案涉及假设一个良好指定的模型族，在这种情况下，存在一个在 p0 和 p* 下进行预测的唯一最优模型。此时，只需注意估计模型中的有限样本方差 [25, 87]。此方法的一个限制（至少目前）是模型在实践中常常被错误指定。然而，这可以通过采用高度表达性的模型族来克服，例如再生核希尔伯特空间 [72]、图灵机 [143, 144] 或足够表达性的神经网络 [64, 79]。对于后者，最近有有趣的工作使用自助法来估计神经网络学习参数中的有限样本变异 [114]；值得更好地理解这种方法是否能在实践中有效用于估计样本外性能，以及局部最小值、缺乏曲率和其他相对于自助法典型设置 [47] 的奇异性如何影响该方法的有效性。

items.en: All of the approaches so far rely on the covariate shift assumption, which is very strong and is also untestable; the latter property is particularly problematic from a safety perspective, since it could lead to silent failures in a machine learning system. Another approach, which does not rely on covariate shift, builds a generative model of the distribution. Rather than assuming that p​(x)𝑝𝑥p(x) changes while p​(y|x)𝑝conditional𝑦𝑥p(y|x) stays the same, we are free to assume other invariants (for instance, that p​(y)𝑝𝑦p(y) changes but p​(x|y)𝑝conditional𝑥𝑦p(x|y) stays the same, or that certain conditional independencies are preserved). An advantage is that such assumptions are typically more testable than the covariate shift assumption (since they do not only involve the unobserved variable y𝑦y). A disadvantage is that generative approaches are even more fragile than discriminative approaches in the presence of model mis-specification — for instance, there is a large empirical literature showing that generative approaches to semi-supervised learning based on maximizing marginal likelihood can perform very poorly when the model is mis-specified [98, 110, 35, 90, 88].
items.zh: 到目前为止的所有方法都依赖于协变量偏移假设，这个假设非常强且不可检验；后一个特性从安全角度来看尤其成问题，因为它可能导致机器学习系统中的无声失败。另一种不依赖协变量偏移的方法是构建分布的生成模型。我们不必假设 p(x) 变化而 p(y|x) 保持不变，而是可以自由假设其他不变量（例如，p(y) 变化但 p(x|y) 保持不变，或某些条件独立性得以保留）。一个优点是这些假设通常比协变量偏移假设更可检验（因为它们不只涉及未观测变量 y）。一个缺点是生成方法在模型错误指定时比判别方法更脆弱——例如，大量实证文献表明，基于最大化边际似然的生成式半监督学习方法在模型错误指定时表现非常差 [98, 110, 35, 90, 88]。

items.en: The approaches discussed above all rely relatively strongly on having a well-specified model family — one that contains the true distribution or true concept. This can be problematic in many cases, since nature is often more complicated than our model family is capable of capturing. As noted above, it may be possible to mitigate this with very expressive models, such as kernels, Turing machines, or very large neural networks, but even here there is at least some remaining problem: for example, even if our model family consists of all Turing machines, given any finite amount of data we can only actually learn among Turing machines up to a given description length, and if the Turing machine describing nature exceeds this length, we are back to the mis-specified regime (alternatively, nature might not even be describable by a Turing machine).
items.zh: 上述讨论的方法都相对强烈地依赖于拥有一个良好指定的模型族——即包含真实分布或真实概念的模型族。这在许多情况下可能成问题，因为自然往往比我们的模型族所能捕捉的更复杂。如上所述，可以通过非常表达性的模型（如核、图灵机或非常大的神经网络）来缓解这一问题，但即使如此，至少仍存在一些问题：例如，即使我们的模型族包含所有图灵机，给定任何有限数据，我们实际上只能学习到给定描述长度以内的图灵机，如果描述自然的图灵机超过此长度，我们就回到了错误指定区域（或者，自然甚至可能无法由图灵机描述）。

items.en: Partially specified models: method of moments, unsupervised risk estimation, causal identification, and limited-information maximum likelihood. Another approach is to take for granted that constructing a fully well-specified model family is probably infeasible, and to design methods that perform well despite this fact. This leads to the idea of partially specified models — models for which assumptions are made about some aspects of a distribution, but for which we are agnostic or make limited assumptions about other aspects. For a simple example, consider a variant of linear regression where we might assume that y=⟨w∗,x⟩+v𝑦superscript𝑤𝑥𝑣y=\langle w^{*},x\rangle+v, where E​[v|x]=0𝐸delimited-[]conditional𝑣𝑥0E[v|x]=0, but we don’t make any further assumptions about the distributional form of the noise v. It turns out that this is already enough to identify the parameters w∗superscript𝑤w^{*}, and that these parameters will minimize the squared prediction error even if the distribution over x𝑥x changes. What is interesting about this example is that w∗superscript𝑤w^{*} can be identified even with an incomplete (partial) specification of the noise distribution.
items.zh: 部分指定模型：矩方法、无监督风险估计、因果识别和有限信息最大似然。另一种方法是认为构建完全良好指定的模型族可能不可行，并设计在此情况下仍能表现良好的方法。这引出了部分指定模型的概念——即对分布的某些方面做出假设，但对其他方面不可知或做出有限假设的模型。举一个简单例子，考虑线性回归的一个变体，其中我们假设 y = <w*, x> + v，且 E[v|x] = 0，但我们不对噪声 v 的分布形式做进一步假设。事实证明，这已足以识别参数 w*，并且即使 x 的分布发生变化，这些参数也将最小化平方预测误差。这个例子有趣之处在于，即使对噪声分布进行不完整（部分）指定，也能识别 w*。

items.en: This insight can be substantially generalized, and is one of the primary motivations for the generalized method of moments in econometrics [68, 123, 69]. The econometrics literature has in fact developed a large family of tools for handling partial specification, which also includes limited-information maximum likelihood and instrumental variables [10, 11, 133, 132].
items.zh: 这一见解可以大幅推广，并且是计量经济学中广义矩方法的主要动机之一 [68, 123, 69]。计量经济学文献实际上已经发展出一大类处理部分指定的工具，还包括有限信息最大似然和工具变量 [10, 11, 133, 132]。

items.en: Returning to machine learning, the method of moments has recently seen a great deal of success for use in the estimation of latent variable models [9]. While the current focus is on using the method of moments to overcome non-convexity issues, it can also offer a way to perform unsupervised learning while relying only on conditional independence assumptions, rather than the strong distributional assumptions underlying maximum likelihood learning [147].
items.zh: 回到机器学习，矩方法最近在潜变量模型估计中取得了巨大成功 [9]。虽然当前焦点是使用矩方法克服非凸性问题，但它也提供了一种进行无监督学习的方式，仅依赖条件独立性假设，而非最大似然学习背后的强分布假设 [147]。

items.en: Finally, some recent work in machine learning focuses only on modeling the distribution of errors of a model, which is sufficient for determining whether a model is performing well or poorly. Formally, the goal is to perform unsupervised risk estimation — given a model and unlabeled data from a test distribution, estimate the labeled risk of the model. This formalism, introduced by [44], has the advantage of potentially handling very large changes between train and test — even if the test distribution looks completely different from the training distribution and we have no hope of outputting accurate predictions, unsupervised risk estimation may still be possible, as in this case we would only need to output a large estimate for the risk. As in [147], one can approach unsupervised risk estimation by positing certain conditional independencies in the distribution of errors, and using this to estimate the error distribution from unlabeled data [39, 170, 121, 74]. Instead of assuming independence, another assumption is that the errors are Gaussian conditioned on the true output y, in which case estimating the risk reduces to estimating a Gaussian mixture model [18]. Because these methods focus only on the model errors and ignore other aspects of the data distribution, they can also be seen as an instance of partial model specification.
items.zh: 最后，机器学习中的一些近期工作仅关注建模模型误差的分布，这足以确定模型表现是好是坏。形式上，目标是执行无监督风险估计——给定一个模型和来自测试分布的未标记数据，估计模型的标记风险。这一形式化由 [44] 引入，其优点在于可能处理训练和测试之间的巨大变化——即使测试分布看起来与训练分布完全不同，且我们无望输出准确预测，无监督风险估计仍可能实现，因为在这种情况下我们只需输出一个大的风险估计。如 [147] 所述，可以通过假设误差分布中的某些条件独立性，并利用这一点从未标记数据估计误差分布来接近无监督风险估计 [39, 170, 121, 74]。另一种假设是误差在给定真实输出 y 的条件下是高斯分布，此时估计风险简化为估计高斯混合模型 [18]。由于这些方法仅关注模型误差而忽略数据分布的其他方面，它们也可被视为部分模型指定的一个实例。

items.en: Training on multiple distributions. One could also train on multiple training distributions in the hope that a model which simultaneously works well on many training distributions will also work well on a novel test distribution. One of the authors has found this to be the case, for instance, in the context of automated speech recognition systems [7]. One could potentially combine this with any of the ideas above, and/or take an engineering approach of simply trying to develop design methodologies that consistently allow one to collect a representative set of training sets and from this build a model that consistently generalizes to novel distributions. Even for this engineering approach, it seems important to be able to detect when one is in a situation that was not covered by the training data and to respond appropriately, and to have methodologies for adequately stress-testing the model with distributions that are sufficiently different from the set of training distributions.
items.zh: 在多个分布上训练。还可以在多个训练分布上训练，希望一个同时在许多训练分布上表现良好的模型也能在新的测试分布上表现良好。其中一位作者发现，例如在自动语音识别系统背景下，情况确实如此 [7]。可以将此与上述任何想法结合，和/或采取工程方法，即尝试开发一致允许收集代表性训练集并从中构建一致泛化到新分布的模型的设计方法论。即使对于这种工程方法，能够检测何时处于训练数据未覆盖的情况并适当响应，以及拥有用与训练集充分不同的分布对模型进行充分压力测试的方法论，似乎也很重要。

items.en: How to respond when out-of-distribution. The approaches described above focus on detecting when a model is unlikely to make good predictions on a new distribution. An important related question is what to do once the detection occurs. One natural approach would be to ask humans for information, though in the context of complex structured output tasks it may be unclear a priori what question to ask, and in time-critical situations asking for information may not be an option. For the former challenge, there has been some recent promising work on pinpointing aspects of a structure that a model is uncertain about [162, 81], as well as obtaining calibration in structured output settings [83], but we believe there is much work yet to be done. For the latter challenge, there is also relevant work based on reachability analysis [93, 100] and robust policy improvement [164], which provide potential methods for deploying conservative policies in situations of uncertainty; to our knowledge, this work has not yet been combined with methods for detecting out-of-distribution failures of a model.
items.zh: 分布外时如何响应。上述方法侧重于检测模型何时不太可能在新分布上做出良好预测。一个重要的相关问题是一旦检测到该做什么。一种自然的方法是向人类询问信息，尽管在复杂结构化输出任务的背景下，可能事先不清楚该问什么问题，并且在时间紧迫的情况下，询问信息可能不可行。对于前一个挑战，最近有一些有希望的工作，用于精确定位模型不确定的结构方面 [162, 81]，以及在结构化输出设置中获得校准 [83]，但我们认为还有很多工作要做。对于后一个挑战，也有基于可达性分析 [93, 100] 和鲁棒策略改进 [164] 的相关工作，这些工作提供了在不确定情况下部署保守策略的潜在方法；据我们所知，这些工作尚未与检测模型分布外失败的方法相结合。

items.en: Beyond the structured output setting, for agents that can act in an environment (such as RL agents), information about the reliability of percepts in uncertain situations seems to have great potential value. In sufficiently rich environments, these agents may have the option to gather information that clarifies the percept (e.g. if in a noisy environment, move closer to the speaker), engage in low-stakes experimentation when uncertainty is high (e.g. try a potentially dangerous chemical reaction in a controlled environment), or seek experiences that are likely to help expose the perception system to the relevant distribution (e.g. practice listening to accented speech). Humans utilize such information routinely, but to our knowledge current RL techniques make little effort to do so, perhaps because popular RL environments are typically not rich enough to require such subtle management of uncertainty. Properly responding to out-of-distribution information thus seems to the authors like an exciting and (as far as we are aware) mostly unexplored challenge for next generation RL systems.
items.zh: 超越结构化输出设置，对于可以在环境中行动的智能体（如 RL 智能体），关于不确定情况下感知可靠性的信息似乎具有巨大的潜在价值。在足够丰富的环境中，这些智能体可能可以选择收集信息以澄清感知（例如，如果在嘈杂环境中，靠近说话者），在不确定性高时进行低风险实验（例如，在受控环境中尝试潜在危险的化学反应），或寻求可能有助于让感知系统暴露于相关分布的体验（例如，练习听带口音的语音）。人类经常利用此类信息，但据我们所知，当前的 RL 技术很少这样做，可能是因为流行的 RL 环境通常不够丰富，不需要如此精细的不确定性管理。因此，对分布外信息做出适当响应，对于作者来说似乎是下一代 RL 系统的一个令人兴奋且（据我们所知）基本未被探索的挑战。

items.en: A unifying view: counterfactual reasoning and machine learning with contracts. Some of the authors have found two viewpoints to be particularly helpful when thinking about problems related to out-of-distribution prediction. The first is counterfactual reasoning [106, 129, 117, 30], where one asks “what would have happened if the world were different in a certain way”? In some sense, distributional shift can be thought of as a particular type of counterfactual, and so understanding counterfactual reasoning is likely to help in making systems robust to distributional shift. We are excited by recent work applying counterfactual reasoning techniques to machine learning problems [30, 120, 151, 160, 77, 137] though there appears to be much work remaining to be done to scale these to high-dimensional and highly complex settings.
items.zh: 统一视角：反事实推理和带合约的机器学习。一些作者发现，在思考与分布外预测相关的问题时，有两个视角特别有帮助。第一个是反事实推理 [106, 129, 117, 30]，其中问“如果世界以某种方式不同，会发生什么？”从某种意义上说，分布偏移可以被视为一种特定类型的反事实，因此理解反事实推理可能有助于使系统对分布偏移具有鲁棒性。我们对最近将反事实推理技术应用于机器学习问题的工作感到兴奋 [30, 120, 151, 160, 77, 137]，尽管似乎还有很多工作要做才能将其扩展到高维和高度复杂的设置。

items.en: The second perspective is machine learning with contracts — in this perspective, one would like to construct machine learning systems that satisfy a well-defined contract on their behavior in analogy with the design of software systems [135, 28, 89]. [135] enumerates a list of ways in which existing machine learning systems fail to do this, and the problems this can cause for deployment and maintenance of machine learning systems at scale. The simplest and to our mind most important failure is the extremely brittle implicit contract in most machine learning systems, namely that they only necessarily perform well if the training and test distributions are identical. This condition is difficult to check and rare in practice, and it would be valuable to build systems that perform well under weaker contracts that are easier to reason about. Partially specified models offer one approach to this — rather than requiring the distributions to be identical, we only need them to match on the pieces of the distribution that are specified in the model. Reachability analysis [93, 100] and model repair [58] provide other avenues for obtaining better contracts — in reachability analysis, we optimize performance subject to the condition that a safe region can always be reached by a known conservative policy, and in model repair we alter a trained model to ensure that certain desired safety properties hold.
items.zh: 第二个视角是带合约的机器学习——在此视角下，我们希望构建满足明确定义的行为合约的机器学习系统，类似于软件系统的设计 [135, 28, 89]。[135] 列举了现有机器学习系统未能做到这一点的多种方式，以及这在大规模部署和维护机器学习系统时可能引起的问题。最简单且我们认为最重要的失败是大多数机器学习系统中极其脆弱的隐式合约，即它们仅在训练和测试分布相同时才必然表现良好。这一条件难以检查且在实践中很少见，构建在更弱且更易推理的合约下表现良好的系统将很有价值。部分指定模型为此提供了一种方法——我们不需要分布相同，只需它们在模型中指定的分布部分匹配。可达性分析 [93, 100] 和模型修复 [58] 提供了获得更好合约的其他途径——在可达性分析中，我们在已知保守策略总能到达安全区域的条件下优化性能；在模型修复中，我们修改训练好的模型以确保某些期望的安全属性成立。

items.en: Summary. There are a variety of approaches to building machine learning systems that robustly perform well when deployed on novel test distributions. One family of approaches is based on assuming a well-specified model; in this case, the primary obstacles are the difficulty of building well-specified models in practice, an incomplete picture of how to maintain uncertainty on novel distributions in the presence of finite training data, and the difficulty of detecting when a model is mis-specified. Another family of approaches only assumes a partially specified model; this approach is potentially promising, but it currently suffers from a lack of development in the context of machine learning, since most of the historical development has been by the field of econometrics; there is also a question of whether partially specified models are fundamentally constrained to simple situations and/or conservative predictions, or whether they can meaningfully scale to the complex situations demanded by modern machine learning applications. Finally, one could try to train on multiple training distributions in the hope that a model which simultaneously works well on many training distributions will also work well on a novel test distribution; for this approach it seems particularly important to stress-test the learned model with distributions that are substantially different from any in the set of training distributions. In addition, it is probably still important to be able to predict when inputs are too novel to admit good predictions.
items.zh: 总结。有多种方法可以构建在新型测试分布上部署时鲁棒表现良好的机器学习系统。一类方法基于假设良好指定的模型；在这种情况下，主要障碍是实践中构建良好指定模型的困难、在有限训练数据下如何在新型分布上保持不确定性的不完整图景，以及检测模型何时被错误指定的困难。另一类方法仅假设部分指定的模型；这种方法有潜力，但目前缺乏在机器学习背景下的发展，因为大部分历史发展来自计量经济学领域；还有一个问题是部分指定模型是否从根本上局限于简单情况和/或保守预测，或者它们能否有意义地扩展到现代机器学习应用所需的复杂情况。最后，可以尝试在多个训练分布上训练，希望一个同时在许多训练分布上表现良好的模型也能在新的测试分布上表现良好；对于这种方法，用与训练分布集充分不同的分布对学习模型进行压力测试似乎特别重要。此外，能够预测输入何时过于新颖而无法做出良好预测可能仍然很重要。

items.en: Potential Experiments: Speech systems frequently exhibit poor calibration when they go out-of-distribution, so a speech system that “knows when it is uncertain” could be one possible demonstration project. To be specific, the challenge could be: train a state-of-the-art speech system on a standard dataset [116] that gives well-calibrated results (if not necessarily good results) on a range of other test sets, like noisy and accented speech. Current systems not only perform poorly on these test sets when trained only on small datasets, but are usually overconfident in their incorrect transcriptions. Fixing this problem without harming performance on the original training set would be a valuable achievement, and would obviously have practical value. More generally, it would be valuable to design models that could consistently estimate (bounds on) their performance on novel test distributions. If a single methodology could consistently accomplish this for a wide variety of tasks (including not just speech but e.g. sentiment analysis [24], as well as benchmarks in computer vision [158]), that would inspire confidence in the reliability of that methodology for handling novel inputs. Note that estimating performance on novel distributions has additional practical value in allowing us to then potentially adapt the model to that new situation. Finally, it might also be valuable to create an environment where an RL agent must learn to interpret speech as part of some larger task, and to explore how to respond appropriately to its own estimates of its transcription error.
items.zh: 潜在实验：语音系统在分布外时经常表现出较差的校准，因此一个“知道何时不确定”的语音系统可能是一个可能的演示项目。具体来说，挑战可能是：在一个标准数据集 [116] 上训练最先进的语音系统，使其在一系列其他测试集（如嘈杂和带口音的语音）上给出良好校准的结果（即使不一定良好）。当前系统仅在小型数据集上训练时，不仅在这些测试集上表现不佳，而且通常对其错误转录过于自信。在不损害原始训练集性能的情况下解决这个问题将是一项有价值的成就，并且显然具有实际价值。更一般地，设计能够一致估计（其性能界限）在新型测试分布上性能的模型将很有价值。如果单一方法能在一系列广泛任务（不仅包括语音，还包括情感分析 [24] 以及计算机视觉基准 [158]）上一致实现这一点，那将增强对该方法处理新型输入可靠性的信心。注意，估计在新型分布上的性能具有额外的实际价值，使我们能够随后潜在地使模型适应新情况。最后，创建一个 RL 智能体必须学习解释语音作为更大任务一部分的环境，并探索如何对其自身转录误差的估计做出适当响应，也可能很有价值。

items.en: As mentioned in the introduction, several other communities have thought broadly about the safety of AI systems, both within and outside of the machine learning community. Work within the machine learning community on accidents in particular was discussed in detail above, but here we very briefly highlight a few other communities doing work that is broadly related to the topic of AI safety.
items.zh: 如引言所述，其他几个社区（包括机器学习社区内外）也广泛思考了 AI 系统的安全性。上文已详细讨论了机器学习社区内关于事故的工作，但在此我们简要强调其他几个从事与 AI 安全广泛相关工作的社区。

items.en: Cyber-Physical Systems Community: An existing community of researchers studies the security and safety of systems that interact with the physical world. Illustrative of this work is an impressive and successful effort to formally verify the entire federal aircraft collision avoidance system [75, 92]. Similar work includes traffic control algorithms [101] and many other topics. However, to date this work has not focused much on modern machine learning systems, where formal verification is often not feasible.
items.zh: 信息物理系统社区：一个现有的研究人员社区研究的是与物理世界交互的系统的安全性和安保性。这方面的一项令人印象深刻且成功的努力是对整个联邦飞机防撞系统进行形式化验证 [75, 92]。类似的工作还包括交通控制算法 [101] 和许多其他主题。然而，迄今为止，这项工作并未过多关注现代机器学习系统，因为在这些系统中形式化验证通常不可行。

items.en: Futurist Community: A cross-disciplinary group of academics and non-profits has raised concern about the long term implications of AI [27, 167], particularly superintelligent AI. The Future of Humanity Institute has studied this issue particularly as it relates to future AI systems learning or executing humanity’s preferences [48, 43, 14, 12]. The Machine Intelligence Research Institute has studied safety issues that may arise in very advanced AI [57, 56, 36, 154, 142], including a few mentioned above (e.g., wireheading, environmental embedding, counterfactual reasoning), albeit at a more philosophical level. To date, they have not focused much on applications to modern machine learning. By contrast, our focus is on the empirical study of practical safety problems in modern machine learning systems, which we believe is likely to be robustly useful across a broad variety of potential risks, both short- and long-term.
items.zh: 未来学家社区：一个跨学科的学者和非营利组织群体对 AI 的长期影响，特别是超级智能 AI 表示担忧 [27, 167]。人类未来研究所研究了这一问题，特别是与未来 AI 系统学习或执行人类偏好相关的问题 [48, 43, 14, 12]。机器智能研究所研究了在非常先进的 AI 中可能出现的安全问题 [57, 56, 36, 154, 142]，包括上文提到的一些问题（例如，导线头、环境嵌入、反事实推理），尽管是在更哲学的层面上。迄今为止，他们并未过多关注现代机器学习的应用。相比之下，我们的重点是实证研究现代机器学习系统中实际的安全问题，我们认为这对于应对各种潜在风险（无论是短期还是长期）都具有稳健的实用性。

items.en: Other Calls for Work on Safety: There have been other public documents within the research community pointing out the importance of work on AI safety. A 2015 Open Letter [8] signed by many members of the research community states the importance of “how to reap [AI’s] benefits while avoiding the potential pitfalls.” [130] propose research priorities for robust and beneficial artificial intelligence, and includes several other topics in addition to a (briefer) discussion of AI-related accidents. [161], writing over 20 years ago, proposes that the community look for ways to formalize Asimov’s first law of robotics (robots must not harm humans), and focuses mainly on classical planning. Finally, two of the authors of this paper have written informally about safety in AI systems [146, 34]; these postings provided inspiration for parts of the present document.
items.zh: 其他关于安全工作的呼吁：研究社区内还有其他公开文件指出了 AI 安全工作的重要性。2015 年由许多研究社区成员签署的公开信 [8] 指出“如何收获 AI 的好处同时避免潜在陷阱”的重要性。[130] 提出了稳健且有益的人工智能的研究重点，除了（更简短的）AI 相关事故讨论外，还包括其他几个主题。[161] 在 20 多年前提出，社区应寻找形式化阿西莫夫机器人第一定律（机器人不得伤害人类）的方法，并主要关注经典规划。最后，本文的两位作者曾非正式地撰写过关于 AI 系统安全性的文章 [146, 34]；这些文章为本文的部分内容提供了灵感。

items.en: Related Problems in Safety: A number of researchers in machine learning and other fields have begun to think about the social impacts of AI technologies. Aside from work directly on accidents (which we reviewed in the main document), there is also substantial work on other topics, many of which are closely related to or overlap with the issue of accidents. A thorough overview of all of this work is beyond the scope of this document, but we briefly list a few emerging themes:
items.zh: 安全中的相关问题：机器学习及其他领域的许多研究人员已经开始思考 AI 技术的社会影响。除了直接关于事故的工作（我们在主文档中已回顾）之外，还有其他主题的大量工作，其中许多与事故问题密切相关或重叠。全面概述所有这些工作超出了本文的范围，但我们简要列出几个新兴主题：

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Privacy: How can we ensure privacy when applying machine learning to sensitive data sources such as medical data? [76, 1]
items.zh: ∙ 隐私：在将机器学习应用于敏感数据源（如医疗数据）时，我们如何确保隐私？[76, 1]

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Fairness: How can we make sure ML systems don’t discriminate? [3, 168, 6, 46, 119, 169]
items.zh: ∙ 公平性：我们如何确保机器学习系统不歧视？[3, 168, 6, 46, 119, 169]

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Security: What can a malicious adversary do to a ML system? [149, 96, 97, 115, 108, 19]
items.zh: ∙ 安全性：恶意对手可以对机器学习系统做什么？[149, 96, 97, 115, 108, 19]

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Abuse:555Note that “security” differs from “abuse” in that the former involves attacks against a legitimate ML system by an adversary (e.g. a criminal tries to fool a face recognition system), while the latter involves attacks by an ML system controlled by an adversary (e.g. a criminal trains a “smart hacker” system to break into a website). How do we prevent the misuse of ML systems to attack or harm people? [16]
items.zh: ∙ 滥用：555注意，“安全性”与“滥用”的区别在于，前者涉及对手对合法机器学习系统的攻击（例如，罪犯试图欺骗人脸识别系统），而后者涉及由对手控制的机器学习系统的攻击（例如，罪犯训练一个“智能黑客”系统来入侵网站）。我们如何防止滥用机器学习系统来攻击或伤害他人？[16]

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Transparency: How can we understand what complicated ML systems are doing? [112, 166, 105, 109]
items.zh: ∙ 透明度：我们如何理解复杂的机器学习系统在做什么？[112, 166, 105, 109]

items.en: ∙∙~{}\bullet~{}Policy: How do we predict and respond to the economic and social consequences of ML? [32, 52, 15, 33]
items.zh: ∙ 政策：我们如何预测和应对机器学习的经济和社会后果？[32, 52, 15, 33]

items.en: We believe that research on these topics has both urgency and great promise, and that fruitful intersection is likely to exist between these topics and the topics we discuss in this paper.
items.zh: 我们认为，对这些主题的研究既紧迫又前景广阔，并且这些主题与本文讨论的主题之间很可能存在富有成效的交集。

items.en: This paper analyzed the problem of accidents in machine learning systems and particularly reinforcement learning agents, where an accident is defined as unintended and harmful behavior that may emerge from poor design of real-world AI systems. We presented five possible research problems related to accident risk and for each we discussed possible approaches that are highly amenable to concrete experimental work.
items.zh: 本文分析了机器学习系统，特别是强化学习智能体中的事故问题，其中事故被定义为由于现实世界AI系统设计不良而可能出现的意外且有害的行为。我们提出了与事故风险相关的五个可能的研究问题，并针对每个问题讨论了高度适合具体实验工作的可能方法。

items.en: With the realistic possibility of machine learning-based systems controlling industrial processes, health-related systems, and other mission-critical technology, small-scale accidents seem like a very concrete threat, and are critical to prevent both intrinsically and because such accidents could cause a justified loss of trust in automated systems. The risk of larger accidents is more difficult to gauge, but we believe it is worthwhile and prudent to develop a principled and forward-looking approach to safety that continues to remain relevant as autonomous systems become more powerful. While many current-day safety problems can and have been handled with ad hoc fixes or case-by-case rules, we believe that the increasing trend towards end-to-end, fully autonomous systems points towards the need for a unified approach to prevent these systems from causing unintended harm.
items.zh: 鉴于基于机器学习的系统控制工业流程、健康相关系统及其他关键任务技术的现实可能性，小规模事故似乎是一个非常具体的威胁，并且对于内在原因以及此类事故可能导致对自动化系统信任的合理丧失而言，预防这些事故至关重要。更大规模事故的风险更难评估，但我们认为，随着自主系统变得更加强大，制定一种有原则且前瞻性的安全方法是值得且谨慎的，这种方法将继续保持相关性。虽然许多当前的安全问题可以通过临时修复或逐案规则来处理，但我们认为，向端到端、完全自主系统发展的趋势表明，需要一种统一的方法来防止这些系统造成意外伤害。
